windows_abdul_status: findCodes4 tests !types[0] twice and never tests !types[3], so selecting CS and ONS can incorrectly dispatch only three VaryCS calls instead of native Vary4 for the mixed request
fix_or_plan: Documented current menu path without changing production code
next_action: Replace the duplicate !types[0] with !types[3]; add one test for every primary type combination around CS and ONS

area: override AutoVary never increases non-CS upper bound
linux_evidence: Found while tracing the per-type AutoVary empty-result iteration
windows_abdul_status: The override AutoVary initializes OSstep to zero then updates it with OSstep += OSstep, leaving it zero forever; after an empty search it repeats the same non-CS upper bound despite the requested step
fix_or_plan: Documented current menu path without changing production code
next_action: Increment OSstep by step or set it from the intended next bound; add empty-first-then-hit fixtures that assert both CS and non-CS ranges advance

area: splitUp output option cannot be safely enabled
linux_evidence: Found while tracing the compile-time false output branch
windows_abdul_status: When static final splitUp is changed to true the branch appends into splitCodes without initialization; the documented configuration flag therefore does not compile or run as intended
fix_or_plan: Documented dormant output path without changing production code
next_action: Initialize splitCodes before the type loop and add output-order tests with splitUp enabled; remove the flag if the mode is intentionally unsupported

area: closing a progress dialog does not request native cancellation
linux_evidence: Found while comparing window-close and Cancel-button handlers
windows_abdul_status: Each Cancel-button handler calls Wrapper.backend_cancel before Java cancellation, but each stage close-request handler only invokes requestCancel; an in-flight native Vary call can continue after its modal window is closed
fix_or_plan: Documented the split cancellation contract without changing production code
validation: Source inspection in handbook Section 14.74; typed menu Vary reaches the native global cancellation lane
next_action: Make close-request invoke the same cancellation helper as the button or explicitly label it hide-only; test a blocking native Vary fixture through button cancel and window-manager close, asserting both request backend cancellation and terminate without artifact loss

area: PolyVary progress is neither visited-coordinate nor submitted-work progress
linux_evidence: Found while tracing every updateProgress branch against todo=coordList.size
windows_abdul_status: PolyVary omits containment skips and double-counts empty/submitted work; CycleVary has the same advertised-coordinate versus work-event mismatch.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Source inspection in handbook Sections 14.75 and 14.78.
next_action: Choose one unit of work and advance exactly once per completed coordinate, or expose distinct discovery and storage totals; add all-skipped empty-candidate submitted-storage and mixed traversal progress tests

area: PolyVary can pair a PixelReader with dimensions from a different image
linux_evidence: Found while tracing the two JavaFX FutureTask snapshot operations
windows_abdul_status: Both tasks read the ImageView PixelReader in one Platform.runLater task and image dimensions in a later task, permitting a redraw to tear the logical snapshot.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Source inspection in handbook Sections 14.75 and 14.78.
next_action: Read Image, PixelReader, width, and height together in one FX runnable into one immutable snapshot; add a redraw-between-requests regression test and an edge-pixel test

area: malformed AutoPolyVary bounds choose inconsistent fallback state
linux_evidence: Found while tracing every bounds-file parse branch
windows_abdul_status: A malformed six-token record resets to 300 100 36 222 222 222, but a short record leaves previous shared static values such as 300 50 36 800 300 150; split on a literal space also turns repeated whitespace into malformed tokens
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Parse exactly six whitespace-delimited integers into a local immutable configuration, validate nonnegative ranges, and use one declared fallback on every malformed/missing file; add short file noninteger repeated-space and reopen-dialog tests

area: AutoPolyVary group-count validation throws for nonempty invalid input
linux_evidence: Found while tracing getNumGroupToPrint into drawAutoPolyVary
windows_abdul_status: Blank and negative group counts display an alert and return null, but a nonempty noninteger or whitespace-padded integer catches NumberFormatException only to throw a new exception; Viewer does not catch it before constructing PolyVaryTask
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Trim before parsing and return the same explicit invalid/null result for every malformed value; add blank negative alphabetic and whitespace-padded field tests through the viewer action

area: CycleVaryTask and VaryLTask clear an external interrupt and return partial work as success
linux_evidence: Found while tracing the candidate-search exception cancellation branches
windows_abdul_status: Each RuntimeException branch tests Thread.interrupted to decide whether to stop; that query clears the worker interrupt and neither task restores it nor transitions to cancelled, so each can return partial results through normal success
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Source inspection in handbook Sections 14.78 and 14.79
next_action: Use Thread.currentThread().isInterrupted for the predicate or restore the flag before returning, then expose an explicit cancelled result; add externally interrupted candidate-search fixtures and assert interrupt preservation plus no success presentation

area: VaryLTask mutates IterateToLimit JavaFX controls from its worker thread
linux_evidence: Found while tracing add-to-cover flags through addToIterToLimitCover
windows_abdul_status: VaryLTask.call invokes IterateToLimitWindow.addToContent directly after computing a pattern, and that method calls TextArea getText/setText; JavaFX control mutation must occur on the application thread
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Compute pattern in the worker then schedule ordered addToContent calls on Platform.runLater; add enabled-all-positive enabled-plus-minus and cancellation-while-queued UI-thread regression tests

area: middle representative cap is unordered and can be exceeded across Vary workflows
linux_evidence: Found while tracing grouped middle selection and the cap decrement in both implementations
windows_abdul_status: Odd-even groups are iterated through HashMap keySet while a global cap is decremented once per group, so capped selection has no declared group order; with firstLast true one admitted group can print and schedule up to three codes despite one cap unit
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Source inspection in handbook Sections 14.79 and 14.80; PrintMid is used by PolyVary/CycleVary
next_action: Use deterministic ordered maps or sorted keys and enforce the cap per emitted code, or rename/document it as a deterministic group budget; add same-input repeated-run and firstLast cap-bound tests for each caller

area: VaryLTask progress total does not match its one-coordinate work
linux_evidence: Found while comparing recursive task scope with todo calculation
windows_abdul_status: Each VaryLTask processes coordList[idx] only, but todo is coordList.size times maxPrint; additionally maxPrint=0 intentionally means all local codes yet creates a zero total while worker loads still increment progress
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Set task total after representative selection for its one coordinate or move the count to the recursive outer progress controller; add one-coordinate multi-coordinate and maxPrint-zero progress assertions

area: PrintMid silently groups incorrectly for unordered collections
linux_evidence: Found while tracing its code-length boundary flush algorithm
windows_abdul_status: The public helper accepts Collection but flushes all group maps whenever the next iterated code length changes; the same type-length-pattern group is split and may emit duplicate representatives when input is not nondecreasing by codeLength
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Sort a defensive copy by codeLength type and odd-even pattern or require a sorted collection type in the API; add interleaved-length and sorted-equivalent tests

area: BatchLoadStorage can block a UI caller for native/database shutdown after failure
linux_evidence: Found while tracing batch future failure through finally shutdown
windows_abdul_status: A failed or cancelled batch cancels all futures then finally calls safeShutdownExecutor with a 30-second synchronous wait; native/database work may not honor interruption promptly, and callers include UI orchestration paths; Future.get interruption is also wrapped without restoring the caller flag
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Run batch aggregation off the FX thread or make shutdown completion asynchronous, restore interrupt, and return explicit cancelled/failed results; add a blocking-storage fixture asserting UI responsiveness and bounded cancellation behavior

area: polygon text parser rejects repeated whitespace and tabs
linux_evidence: Found while deriving the exact cleanPolygon token grammar
windows_abdul_status: After punctuation removal cleanPolygon uses split on a literal single space and requires exactly two tokens, so repeated spaces create empty tokens and tabs do not separate coordinates even though both forms are visually normal polygon input
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Tokenize trimmed coordinates with whitespace-plus, validate exact numeric pairs, and return an actionable parse result; add single-space repeated-space tab comma-parenthesis and blank-input tests

area: Iterate-to-Limit restores plus/minus text into the wrong lane
linux_evidence: Found while comparing constructor slot assignments against saveContentsToFile order
windows_abdul_status: saveContentsToFile writes pmStables at delimiter slot 2 and pmUnstables at slot 4, but the constructor writes both slots into pmStablesTextArea and never restores pmUnstablesTextArea; reopening and saving destroys former pm-stable content and migrates pm-unstable content
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Assign contents[4] to pmUnstablesTextArea and add a seven-field save reopen save round-trip test with distinct lane text

area: Iterate-to-Limit treats missing storage components as an intersecting code family
linux_evidence: Found while comparing expected classified-component count with returned storage cardinality
windows_abdul_status: Base validation tests intersection only against the returned list so an empty list passes vacuously; later iteration sets a stop flag for a short list but still appends it when every surviving storage intersects, permitting incomplete singles/triples to reach Viewer drawing and cover insertion
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Require returned storage count to equal classified component count before any intersection test and never publish a partial triple; add missing-base missing-later-component and complete-triple fixtures

area: Iterate-to-Limit invalid input can leave its running latch set or throw from the UI action
linux_evidence: Found while tracing every exit after run sets running true
windows_abdul_status: Polygon.cleanPolygon or createConvexPolygon can throw after running=true with no catch/finally, so later Run clicks only report an already-running task; a noninteger limit clears the latch but throws RuntimeException instead of showing the validation alert
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Validate polygon and limit before acquiring the running state or clear it in finally, convert expected parse/geometry failures into visible errors, and add invalid-polygon noninteger-limit then valid-rerun UI tests

area: Iterate-to-Limit blocks the JavaFX event thread while it waits worker futures
linux_evidence: Found while following the Run button action through run and Future.get
windows_abdul_status: The Run button calls run directly on the FX action thread; although per-line iteration tasks use an executor, run synchronously waits every Future.get before returning, freezing repaint close and cancellation for the full database/native workload
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Move orchestration/aggregation to a JavaFX Task or background coordinator and publish immutable results on the FX thread; add a delayed-storage responsiveness and close/cancel test

area: Iteration pattern positions are validated too late and fail as opaque worker exceptions
linux_evidence: Found by comparing numeric-only pattern parsing with calcCodeNumbers array indexing
windows_abdul_status: iterateTask accepts any integer pattern token, but calcCodeNumbers turns zero into index minus one and permits out-of-range absolute positions to throw inside a submitted future; run logs an ExecutionException and silently omits that pair instead of identifying the bad token
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Before task submission require every signed position to be nonzero and have absolute value within its code component length; add zero too-large negative-in-range and repeated-position tests

area: Whitespace-only polygon text is not treated as a documented blank selection
linux_evidence: Found while tracing each exact-isEmpty polygon branch through the shared polygon parser
windows_abdul_status: IterationPolyWindow treats whitespace-only text as malformed input rather than Optional.empty, while PolyLoad treats it as malformed input rather than its documented current-screen fallback; both pass an empty cleaned string into createConvexPolygon and their Viewer callers do not catch this input failure
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Source inspection in handbook Sections 14.84 and 14.91
next_action: Use trim().isEmpty for the relevant no-polygon/fallback state and expose malformed/geometry failures as typed validation results; add empty whitespace malformed nonconvex valid-degree and PolyLoad-current-screen tests

area: Pattern Calculator continues after a rejected component and can print a partial recipe
linux_evidence: Found while tracing every alert/return path from calcSequencePattern into calcCodePattern
windows_abdul_status: calcSequencePattern shows an alert for mismatched lengths but keeps indexing, and returns an empty string for parse or odd-difference errors; calcCodePattern still appends that blank component, continues other triple components, and prints a potentially partial/malformed pattern
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Make component calculation return typed success/failure, check equal lengths before indexing, abort the whole calculation on any failure, and add shorter longer nonnumeric odd-difference and mixed-validity triple tests

area: Pattern Calculator has no visible result or transfer path in the GUI
linux_evidence: Found while tracing the Calculate Pattern action's only output side effect
windows_abdul_status: A successful calculation prints only to System.out; it neither updates the window nor copies/saves/inserts the inferred pattern, so packaged GUI users can receive no observable result and cannot directly move it into LiPattern
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Render a labelled result field and provide explicit copy and add-to-LiPattern actions; add an integration test that asserts result visibility and the exact transferred text for singles and triples

area: Pattern Lookup enables Copy when no database result is selected
linux_evidence: Found while following an empty query through selectFirst and the Copy action
windows_abdul_status: An empty exact or odd-even query leaves the ListView selection null but the constructor stores that null in selectedPattern and leaves Copy enabled; the button passes the null value to ClipboardContent.putString with no empty-result state or guard
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Disable Copy until a non-null row is selected and render a no-matches state; add empty exact empty parity first-result and selection-change clipboard tests

area: Pattern Lookup runs database queries synchronously on the JavaFX selection listener
linux_evidence: Found while tracing ComboBox selection callbacks into legacy Database lookup methods
windows_abdul_status: Selecting Code Sequence or Odd-Even Pattern invokes Database.lookUpIterPatByCodeSeq or lookUpIterPatByOEPat directly from the JavaFX event handler, so SQLite latency/contention blocks repaint close and copy processing; an empty result is also re-queried on every reselection
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Query off the FX thread, publish immutable results through Platform.runLater, distinguish empty success from failure, and cache completed empty results; add delayed/busy database responsiveness and reselection tests

area: PolyTrimmer derives a corrupted output name from .txt inputs
linux_evidence: Found while tracing Trim File output-path construction
windows_abdul_status: Trim File calls String.split on the regex .txt, whose wildcard dot consumes the character preceding txt; a source named file.txt can be exported as fil_PolyTrim.txt rather than file_PolyTrim.txt, and other paths with txt are similarly unstable
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Use Path/file-name extension handling or a quoted suffix replacement and add ordinary multi-dot no-extension and directory-name-txt path tests

area: PolyTrimmer aborts an entire file trim when one code has no stored region
linux_evidence: Found while tracing each parsed code through Database.loadStorage
windows_abdul_status: Trim File calls Database.loadStorage(code,pool).get without checking Optional presence, so one valid classified code with no calculated storage throws and prevents final output without identifying the offending code or exposing an empty/backend-failure state
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Handle Optional state per code, report absent/failed rows, and define complete-failure versus annotated-partial export; add mixed present/absent code fixtures

area: PolyTrimmer performs database and native cover trimming on the JavaFX event thread
linux_evidence: Found while following all three button actions from their JavaFX handlers
windows_abdul_status: Trim File synchronously reloads every code's storage and Trim Cover directly invokes the native cover trimmer from the modal UI handler, freezing repaint close and cancellation for potentially long workloads
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Move trim computation/export to a cancellable background task, capture immutable polygon/input data, and publish typed completion/failure on the FX thread; add delayed-storage and blocking-native responsiveness tests

area: PolyVaryLoad retains a closed dialog for every cover-polygon listener it installs
linux_evidence: Found while tracing construction and Stage close behavior
windows_abdul_status: Each PolyVaryLoad constructor adds a CoverWindow.polyStringProperty listener that captures its text area, but neither successful submit nor close removes it. Reopening the modal repeatedly retains closed UI state and adds redundant callbacks on every cover update.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Keep the listener handle and unregister it on every terminal dialog path, or bind only for the dialog lifetime; test repeated open-close cycles and bounded callback/listener counts.

area: PolyVaryLoad partially applies malformed persisted bounds
linux_evidence: Found while tracing updateBounds token parsing and assignment order
windows_abdul_status: updateBounds splits only on literal spaces and assigns six global limits sequentially inside a NumberFormatException-swallowing block. A malformed later token leaves an unannounced mix of newly parsed and old values; whitespace layouts can also fail to tokenize as intended.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Split general whitespace, parse and validate a complete local six-value tuple, then assign atomically; report malformed files and add malformed-at-every-position plus whitespace and restart tests.

area: Code-and-pattern lookup hides SQL failures and skips executor cleanup
linux_evidence: Found while tracing the asynchronous JDBC lookup failure branch
windows_abdul_status: A SQLException schedules only isLoading=false on the JavaFX thread and then throws a RuntimeException on the worker. The user receives no failure state or retry feedback, and executor.shutdown is bypassed because it is after the try/catch.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Use try/finally to terminate the executor and publish a typed lookup failure/retry state on the JavaFX thread; add unavailable-database and malformed-schema tests.

area: Code-and-pattern lookup can launch duplicate initial pages
linux_evidence: Found while comparing show initial-load condition with the isLoading gate
windows_abdul_status: show starts an initial request whenever data is empty but does not require !isLoading. Re-showing or clicking Lookup while the first page is in flight starts another OFFSET 0 query; both can append duplicates and advance currentOffset twice, causing later pages to skip rows.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Gate initial load on !isLoading or one authoritative load future/state machine; test rapid show calls before completion and assert exactly one first-page query and correct offset.

area: Code-and-pattern lookup repeatedly queries an exhausted final page
linux_evidence: Found while tracing zero-row page completion through the scroll listener
windows_abdul_status: A final page with fewer than the requested rows, including zero, leaves currentOffset unchanged and records no hasMore state. Later scrolls across the 0.95 threshold issue the same exhausted OFFSET query again.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Record end-of-data for a short page, disable/remove further load-on-scroll until explicit refresh, and test zero exact-multiple and partial-final result sets.

area: Automatic cover color cycling discards most manually selectable colors
linux_evidence: Found while comparing the 144-color picker palette with ColorPicker.next
windows_abdul_status: ColorPicker.next cycles only its 12-color first row; any other non-Black color maps to Black on the next automatic cycle advance. The picker offers many such non-cycle colors without warning, so an enabled cover cycle silently replaces a user's manual choice.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Either make the cycle include all permitted nontransparent selections or make the short-cycle restriction visible; add primary-cycle Black and arbitrary manual-color transition tests.

area: CoverInfo reads and concatenates complete cover artifacts on the JavaFX event thread
linux_evidence: Found while tracing the CoverInfo button through constructor-time file reads
windows_abdul_status: The Viewer's FX handler constructs CoverInfoWindow, whose constructor synchronously reads, trims, concatenates, and assigns polygon, square, stable, triple, cover, info, unused, and precision files before showing a Stage. Large cover files block repaint close and input.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Load bounded metadata and requested content in a cancellable background task, page/stream large artifacts, and publish progress or error state on the FX thread; add large-file responsiveness tests.

area: CoverInfo can display a torn multi-file cover generation
linux_evidence: Found while tracing eight independent artifact reads with no publication protocol
windows_abdul_status: CoverInfo reads the working cover files one at a time without a manifest, generation id, reader lock, or atomic directory snapshot. A concurrent cover publisher can make it concatenate old and new artifacts or a partially written file while presenting the result as one cover.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Publish complete cover generations through staging plus atomic rename or an explicit manifest/read lock; test a paused writer between artifacts and assert complete-generation or in-progress results only.

area: CoverWindow throws raw numeric parsing failures on the JavaFX action path
linux_evidence: Found while tracing Calculate from saveToFile through integer conversion
windows_abdul_status: Calculate saves the editable configuration and then converts digits, magnifications, and empty-square text with Integer.parseInt. A malformed value throws RuntimeException instead of identifying the field and leaving a recoverable validation state.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Parse into locals before persistence, validate domains, and render an in-window error for the offending field; add malformed, overflow, zero, and negative-input tests.

area: Closing CoverWindow does not cancel an active native cover calculation
linux_evidence: Found while tracing Stage close behavior against the background Task lifecycle
windows_abdul_status: MRR and All calculations run in daemon JavaFX Tasks, but no task reference, cancellation token, or native cancel operation is retained. Closing the modeless stage only saves text; the worker can continue and later reload/publish cover results after the window is hidden.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Define close semantics, retain a cancellable task/native token, disable or replace close while cancellation is pending, and test close-during-MRR plus close-during-All completion behavior.

area: MRR cover Task reads mutable stable and triple fields after submission
linux_evidence: Found while tracing Calculate's captured values into Task.call
windows_abdul_status: The MRR worker reads stablesString and triplesString fields rather than immutable strings captured when Calculate is pressed. Editing the still-open dialog or triggering its mouse-exit save can change those fields while the worker starts, combining a prior polygon/numeric request with later code text.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Capture all polygon, stable, triple, and numeric input in final local snapshots before constructing the Task; add a barrier test that edits/saves after submit and asserts the native request is unchanged.

area: Cover/Stables cleanHalfTriples discard later three-component lines
linux_evidence: Found while tracing the three-component branches of the duplicated parsers
windows_abdul_status: When a stable/unstable/unstable line is encountered, each cleanHalfTriples copy appends one result and returns from inside the loop, silently ignoring every following line. CoverWindow's copy is dormant; StablesWindow actively calls its copy with one generated line at a time, so current callers mask the multi-line defect.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Source inspection in handbook Sections 14.98 and 14.119
next_action: Replace duplicated parsers with one typed line-numbered parser and process all lines before returning; add multiple-three-component and mixed-line tests through both helpers.

area: Parallel CoverWindow stable cleaning produces nondeterministic output ordering
linux_evidence: Found while tracing ForkJoin parallel completion into synchronized StringBuilder append
windows_abdul_status: cleanStables admits small codes in parallel and appends each completion under a lock. The lock prevents interleaved characters but preserves completion order, not input order; all parallel results also precede later sequential results. Identical input can therefore yield different stable-file order across runs or worker counts.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Compute in parallel with stable input indexes and assemble in input order after completion; test byte-identical output across repeated runs and worker counts.

area: CoverWindow rebuilds large cover annotations on the JavaFX success callback
linux_evidence: Found while tracing MRR and All Task success handlers into redoInfo
windows_abdul_status: After native cover work succeeds, the JavaFX callback calls redoInfo, which reads, splits, maps, rebuilds, and rewrites cover/info.txt. Large cover reports can freeze input and repaint despite the native calculation itself running off-thread.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Move annotation reconstruction and artifact publication to the worker phase, then publish one complete generation and notify/reload on JavaFX; add large-info responsiveness tests.

area: Dormant CoverWindow2 reports MRR success without invoking a cover wrapper
linux_evidence: Found while tracing its live MRR branch past half-triple cleaning
windows_abdul_status: CoverWindow2 cleans and may save half-triple components, but every native wrapper call in its MRR branch is commented out. The handler still reloads a cover, saves, redoes info, and closes, so re-enabling the currently commented Viewer constructor would create a false-success workflow using an old cover.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Do not reconnect the prototype until one explicit native wrapper path, output namespace, and typed success/failure contract are implemented and tested.

area: Dormant CoverWindow2 invokes All cover construction directly on JavaFX
linux_evidence: Found while tracing the All branch of the retained prototype
windows_abdul_status: The prototype calls Wrapper.coverWrapperAll synchronously from its Calculate event handler, without a Task, disable state, cancellation, or failure UI. It is dormant now but would freeze the application immediately if the commented Viewer construction were restored.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Reuse the verified background-task design only after fixing the prototype MRR path; test delayed native All work, failure, close, and cancellation semantics before enabling it.

area: CycleVary does not restore persisted code-length bounds
linux_evidence: Found while tracing constructor reads against Load's nine-value write
windows_abdul_status: The bounds file writes three code-length maxima followed by three side-sum maxima and three increments, but the constructor restores only side sums and parts of the increment state. Its length maxima remain static defaults or stale values from a prior instance despite a present persisted value.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Parse all nine values into one immutable configuration, validate it, then publish every field atomically; add a fresh-process round-trip test.

area: Cycle/Super Vary read bound tokens instead of their step files
linux_evidence: Found while tracing both stepTokens length guards and indexed parses
windows_abdul_status: When a separate step file contains three tokens, each constructor conditionally parses indexes zero through two of boundTokens into side-sum step fields. Actual step tokens are not read, so code-length maxima can overwrite correctly loaded increments.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Source inspection in handbook Sections 14.103 and 14.120
next_action: Remove the obsolete split format or parse stepTokens into a complete local step tuple before atomic assignment; test distinct bounds/steps for both windows and process restarts.

area: CycleVary accepts zero or negative repetition counts
linux_evidence: Found while following Load parsing into the outer state-machine listener
windows_abdul_status: Load accepts any integer for Reps. CycleVaryFunction later computes step modulo Reps, so zero throws ArithmeticException and negative values define incoherent cycle boundaries after the window has closed and work has begun.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Validate one immutable run configuration before launch, requiring positive repetitions and sensible nonnegative/count constraints; add zero and negative input tests.

area: Cycle/Super Vary unbox invalid magnification after alerting
linux_evidence: Found while tracing both completion controllers through getMagnification
windows_abdul_status: Each getter shows an alert and returns null for a nonpositive value; its caller immediately unboxes to primitive double, causing NullPointerException. Malformed text throws raw RuntimeException, and NaN/Infinity can pass the <=0 check into PixelRadianMap state.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Source inspection in handbook Sections 14.103 and 14.120
next_action: Parse once at request validation into an OptionalDouble/typed finite positive result and never launch before success; test malformed, zero, negative, NaN, Infinity, and valid magnification.

area: CycleVary validates line range before clamping the end
linux_evidence: Found while deriving getStartStepEnd's returned triple
windows_abdul_status: A start/end pair can pass start <= end before end is clamped to the coordinate-list length. The returned tuple may then have start greater than end, producing invalid traversal state rather than a rejected user range.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Clamp first and validate 1 <= start <= end <= coordinate count with a positive step; test starts beyond the coordinate list.

area: CycleVary can execute malformed or blank coordinate lines
linux_evidence: Found while tracing OBO import, list-length calculation, and moveScreenToLine
windows_abdul_status: The import path stores raw text without coordinate validation; a blank editor counts as one split line, while navigation splits selected text on one literal space and indexes two tokens directly. Blank lines, tabs, repeated spaces, and malformed rows can throw instead of reporting a line error.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Parse imported/editor coordinates once into typed indexed pairs and make navigation and execution consume that parse; add blank, tab, repeated-space, and malformed-line tests.

area: CycleVary Use Reps checkbox changes only progress display
linux_evidence: Found while comparing the checkbox use to outer recurrence arithmetic
windows_abdul_status: Use Reps controls only the displayed total in repsProgress. The modulo divisor, state transitions, and actual number of repetitions always use Reps, so the control presents a behavioral option that does not alter execution.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Either remove the option or make the run configuration branch the actual repetition recurrence; test both selected states.

area: CycleVary performs native hole probing on the JavaFX state listener
linux_evidence: Found while tracing a repetition boundary after CycleVaryTask success
windows_abdul_status: The JavaFX property listener cleans cover content, may calculate or load database storage, invokes Wrapper.getNotFilledCoordinates, and reloads cover state before advancing. Large cover/native work therefore blocks input and rendering despite candidate Vary work using executors.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Snapshot cover input, run cleaning and native hole probing in a cancellable worker task, and publish only the next coordinate list/state transition on JavaFX; add delayed-native responsiveness tests.

area: CycleVary permits overlapping runs over shared mutable state
linux_evidence: Found while tracing stage closure and outer executor ownership
windows_abdul_status: Starting a run closes rather than disables the stage, while its outer executor and property state machine continue. There is no running latch or completion ownership; reopening can begin another run sharing static bounds, cover text, Viewer images/maps, native pool, and coordinate persistence.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Use one immutable run object with a single owner, explicit cancel/await semantics, and disabled or relabeled launch control until final completion; test rapid reentry.

area: CycleVary strands its outer run after later invalid coordinate range input
linux_evidence: Found while tracing autoCycleVaryFunction's null range return
windows_abdul_status: If a new cycle's editable coordinate state yields an invalid Start/Step/End tuple, autoCycleVaryFunction returns immediately. Its outer executor and both outer progress windows receive neither cancellation nor a final state transition.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Convert range validation to a typed completion that shuts down or keeps the run visibly paused for correction; add invalid-after-first-cycle tests.

area: CycleVary traversal direction is coupled to AutoPolyVaryLoad
linux_evidence: Found while tracing drawCycleVary's recursion predicate
windows_abdul_status: CycleVary accepts forward start/step/end controls but selects its recursion condition from AutoPolyVaryLoad.Reverse, a static flag belonging to another window. If that flag is true, an ordinary forward range commonly stops after one coordinate.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Give CycleVary an explicit local direction and derive its signed index transition from one immutable request; test both directions independently of AutoPolyVaryLoad.

area: CycleVary final storages ignore the repetition color
linux_evidence: Found while comparing partial-results and final-success storage insertion
windows_abdul_status: Partial results use the requested per-repetition color when Color Cycle is enabled, but the final-success catch-up loop always uses Viewer.cycle. A single repetition may therefore draw streamed and terminal results in different colors.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Factor color selection into one shared function used for partial, final, and cancellation paths; test a task that publishes both partial and terminal storage.

area: DBGui accepts database names that escape the database directory
linux_evidence: Found while tracing newDatabase validation into Admin.getDatabasePath
windows_abdul_status: New validates only nonempty/trimmed text and current-list duplication. Admin concatenates that text into the intended database directory without normalization or separator rejection, so dot segments or path separators can designate a native SQLite target outside the configured root.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Accept only a strict basename grammar, resolve/normalize against the database root, require containment before native calls, and test dot-segment and separator names.

area: DBGui runs database administration and exposes failures on JavaFX
linux_evidence: Found while tracing New, Delete, and Clear event handlers
windows_abdul_status: Directory enumeration, Java NIO deletion, and native SQLite create/clear all execute directly in JavaFX handlers. IO or native failures are rethrown as RuntimeException and are not converted to an owned UI failure state; slow or locked storage can freeze the modal chooser.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Run administrative work in cancellable tasks, disable conflicting controls, capture a request generation/name, and publish a refreshed list or visible error on JavaFX; test delayed and failing operations.

area: DBGui Pattern Finder selection is unreachable
linux_evidence: Found while tracing constructor layout against getProgram and Main.start
windows_abdul_status: Both lines that add Viewer and Pattern Finder radio buttons to programBox are commented out. Viewer remains selected by default, so Main's PatternFinder branch has no reachable choice through the database chooser.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Restore and test the controls with clear program-selection semantics, or remove the hidden branch and make Viewer the explicit only startup target.

area: DontDrawPictureTask reports failure after non-atomic database saves
linux_evidence: Found while tracing concurrent callable submission, native save, and first failure handling
windows_abdul_status: The task submits every code as an independent native save before waiting. If one Future fails, earlier and concurrently completing saves may already be durable, but the Task returns only Void and reports a single failed operation with no per-code receipt or resume contract.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Define batch atomicity in the native/database layer or return a durable per-code outcome ledger of saved, empty, cancelled, and failed items; test partial success followed by failure.

area: Viewer runs DrawPictureTask synchronously on JavaFX in iteration paths
linux_evidence: Found while tracing iteration helpers through Utils.runAndWait(Task)
windows_abdul_status: DrawPictureTask submits code loads to an inner executor but its call method waits Future.get. Several Viewer paths pass the Task to Utils.runAndWait, which runs immediately on the JavaFX thread when called from an event handler; repaint, cancel handling, queued partial publication, and terminal callbacks then wait for every ordered future.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Execute the Task from a background coordinator or Viewer executor, retain Progress/cancellation wiring, and publish only final UI mutation on JavaFX; add delayed-load responsiveness and partial-before-final tests.

area: DrawPictureTaskTriples returns null components for empty triple members
linux_evidence: Found while tracing Either.left aggregation into Viewer triple success handler
windows_abdul_status: The task treats a three-entry result as complete even when one or more entries are Either.left. It adds a fixed Storage[3] with unassigned null slots; Viewer then dereferences every slot for intersection and cover formatting on JavaFX.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Validate exactly three input members and return a triple only if all three results are right; represent empty/incomplete triples explicitly and test every empty-component position.

area: DrawPictureTaskTriples counts components against a triple total
linux_evidence: Found while comparing todo initialization to inner aggregation increment
windows_abdul_status: todo is the number of submitted triples, but progress increments once per component while processing each complete triple. A three-component workload can report 3N completed out of N and reach a bound progress bar early.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Use one unit consistently: increment once per completed triple or set total to all component operations; add exact progress tests for multiple triples.

area: DrawPictureTaskUseLR indexes an absent base code
linux_evidence: Found while tracing constructor initialization of its base route
windows_abdul_status: The constructor unconditionally reads classCodeSeqs.get(0) to select a base left-right route. An empty task request throws an index exception before a JavaFX Task state, progress dialog, or validation result exists.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Validate nonempty left-right requests at the caller and fail constructor misuse with a clear IllegalArgumentException; test every LR mode with empty input.

area: DrawPictureTaskUseLRTest chooses reuse bases by completion timing
linux_evidence: Found while tracing concurrent submission against synchronized evolving baseCodeSeq
windows_abdul_status: Every target starts concurrently and snapshots the bases available at its own start time; successful slow fallbacks add new bases only when they complete. The same input can therefore attempt different LR bases, cache invalidation/rebuild paths, and native work under different schedules, even though verifyInfo protects accepted results.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Use an ordered dependency frontier or immutable wavefront bases before parallel target evaluation; test identical input under forced different completion orders.

area: GradientWindow leaves stale successful output after failure
linux_evidence: Found while tracing showResult exception handling and resultArea mutation
windows_abdul_status: The Calculate handler catches every parse/native exception and prints only to the terminal. It does not clear or mark resultArea, so a failed calculation after a successful one leaves the old report visibly associated with the new input fields.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Clear or mark the result pending before calculation and publish a typed visible error; test valid input followed by malformed coordinates and equation.

area: GradientWindow runs symbolic native differentiation on JavaFX
linux_evidence: Found while tracing Calculate action directly into calculate_gradient
windows_abdul_status: The JavaFX action handler synchronously invokes native equation parsing, symbolic differentiation, bounds, and numerical evaluation. Large expressions or slow native failures prevent repaint, input, and close processing.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Capture equation/mode/point in an immutable request, execute native work in a Task, and publish only the matching generation on JavaFX; add delayed-native responsiveness tests.

area: Gradient and Info stages are unowned by Viewer shutdown
linux_evidence: Found while tracing the diagnostic buttons' temporary new-window expressions
windows_abdul_status: Every click constructs a new Stage and Viewer retains no reference or registry entry. Main-window shutdown cannot explicitly close surviving diagnostic windows, which can keep JavaFX alive under implicit-exit semantics.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Source inspection in handbook Sections 14.112, 14.114, and 14.115
next_action: Own reusable diagnostic windows or register all auxiliary stages with Viewer shutdown; test multiple windows followed by primary close.

area: HashTriple uses one overwritten fallback color for all cover categories
linux_evidence: Found while tracing Abdul's default-color memory optimization across bulk add methods
windows_abdul_status: Bulk add methods no longer populate per-rectangle colors; each overwrites one shared defaultColor. Adding stables with one color and triples or half-triples with another recolors every category lacking an explicit override. clear also retains the last fallback.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Store category-specific defaults or one explicit cover-generation color contract; test different stable/triple/half colors and clear/reload.

area: HashTriple bulk add conditionally aliases and mutates caller maps
linux_evidence: Found while tracing MutableMap adoption and the later non-MutableMap branch
windows_abdul_status: An Eclipse MutableMap is adopted by reference. A later ordinary Map load clears and repopulates the currently referenced map, which may be the previous caller's object; external mutations are also immediately visible and unsafe during iteration. The add API does not declare ownership transfer.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Always copy into private final maps, or formalize move ownership and allocate a detached map for copy branches; test adoption, caller mutation, later immutable load, clear, and concurrent enumeration.

area: InfoWindow loads native MRR information on JavaFX
linux_evidence: Found while tracing Show directly into Wrapper.loadInfo
windows_abdul_status: The JavaFX Show handler synchronously enters the native MRR/database information path. A cold or difficult code prevents repaint, close, input, and visible progress until native work returns or fails.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Run loadInfo in a cancellable Task with immutable code/pool input and publish the decoded report or typed failure on JavaFX; add delayed-native responsiveness tests.

area: InfoWindow retains a previous code report after empty or failed load
linux_evidence: Found while tracing non-success branches after an earlier text assignment
windows_abdul_status: Optional.empty shows an alert but does not clear the text area, and an uncaught backend failure also leaves it untouched. The current input can therefore be displayed beside a prior code's successful MRR report.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Clear or mark the report pending before launch and commit only a generation-matched success/error/empty state; test success followed by empty and failure.

area: InfoWindow2 evaluates every equation at x,x instead of x,y
linux_evidence: Found while tracing coordinate fields into infoString and calculateGradient2
windows_abdul_status: showInfo assigns both x_string and y_string from the X TextField. The visible Y value is ignored, so every native gradient/radius result is evaluated at the wrong point whenever x differs from y.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Read and validate the Y field independently, show the exact point in the report, and test an asymmetric equation at x != y.

area: InfoWindow2 runs O(equations) native diagnostics on JavaFX
linux_evidence: Found while tracing Show through dual record loads and per-equation calculateGradient2
windows_abdul_status: One JavaFX action synchronously loads Info and InfoAll, then invokes native symbolic differentiation/evaluation for every selected and all-equation entry. UI latency is the sum of two MRR/database calls and all equation calls, with no cancel or progress path.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Use a background coordinator with immutable input and bounded native concurrency, then publish one generation-tagged report; test large delayed equation sets and close/recalculate.

area: InfoWindow2 calls missing InfoAll a mathematically empty code
linux_evidence: Found while tracing the combined Optional condition
windows_abdul_status: The report is produced only when both loadInfo and loadAllEquation are present. If basic Info exists but the all-equations record is absent, the window displays 'Code Sequence Reduced to Empty Set,' conflating missing auxiliary data with an empty MRR.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Represent basic-info empty, auxiliary-data unavailable, backend failure, and complete diagnostic separately; still display available basic Info.

area: InfoWindow2 minimum summaries use unreachable and cross-collection conditions
linux_evidence: Found while tracing four minimum sentinels through the two footer branches
windows_abdul_status: The positive-MRR branch is hidden by an else-if whenever any positive value also updates the general minimum. The all-equations footer tests MRR sentinels while printing all-equation values, so it can omit real minima or print the untouched 10000 sentinel.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Use separate OptionalDouble accumulators per collection, explicitly select finite or positive semantics, and test empty, NaN, negative, positive, and independently populated collections.

area: QueryStage exposes Search but every click throws disabled exception
linux_evidence: Found while tracing the visible Search command into query
windows_abdul_status: The complete search form is enabled, but query's first live statement throws RuntimeException('searching is currently disabled'). Its former validation/query/draw body is block-commented and references a Search API absent from current production source.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Disable or hide the command with an explicit status, or rebuild the search service and execute it asynchronously before exposing the form; test the presented disabled/restored state.

area: SaveV3Window ignores its editable destination field
linux_evidence: Found while comparing saveToField edits with the location used by saveM, saveL, and clear
windows_abdul_status: The displayed path field is editable but never copied or bound to location. Typing a new path changes only the display; append and destructive clear continue to operate on the last browsed or default path.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Use one authoritative path: make the field read-only or parse and validate its text for every command; integration-test typed and browsed paths, especially clear.

area: SaveV3Window count parsing escapes and negative counts report success
linux_evidence: Found while enumerating Integer.parseInt and prefix-loop branches
windows_abdul_status: Malformed, whitespace, and overflowing input throws uncaught NumberFormatException on JavaFX. Zero and negative integers satisfy count <= size, append no entries, close the stage, and appear successful.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Trim and parse into a validated request with an explicit 1 <= count <= size contract, visible typed errors, and boundary tests.

area: SaveV3Window's new-file write-all branch is unreachable
linux_evidence: Found while tracing FileWriter construction before File.createNewFile
windows_abdul_status: FileWriter(path,true) creates a missing destination before createNewFile is called, so createNewFile always returns false after a successful open. Missing files receive only the requested prefix rather than every entry promised by the dead branch, and an invalid oversized count can leave an empty created file.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Choose one explicit create/append/replace contract, validate before opening, and test missing and existing destinations independently.

area: SaveV3Window can leak writers and partially commit failed exports
linux_evidence: Found while following IOException exits from saveM and saveL
windows_abdul_status: Writers are manually closed only on normal success and the explicit count-too-large path. IOException from write, append, or close enters the catch without a finally close, potentially retaining the descriptor and leaving a partially written prefix.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Validate first, use try-with-resources with an explicit charset, and decide whether append-prefix partial commits or atomic replacement is the required contract; inject write and close failures.

area: SaveV3Window performs filesystem mutation on JavaFX
linux_evidence: Found while tracing all button handlers directly into FileWriter open/write/truncate
windows_abdul_status: Save Matching, Save Latest Vary, and Clear synchronously open and mutate files on the JavaFX application thread. Slow local, network, blocked, or failing destinations prevent repaint, input, and close handling until I/O returns.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Capture a validated immutable request and result-list snapshot on JavaFX, perform scoped file I/O in a Task, and publish only the matching completion; test with a delayed filesystem adapter.

area: SmallCoverWindow runs the complete cover batch on JavaFX
linux_evidence: Found while tracing Calculate through cleaning, database materialization, native cover, artifact reads, and Viewer reload
windows_abdul_status: Every accepted rectangle synchronously re-cleans codes, may calculate missing MRR records, runs native subdivision, writes/reads cover artifacts, and requests rendering in the JavaFX action handler. UI latency is the serial sum and there is no cancellation or progress state.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Capture and validate one immutable batch, clean once, run isolated cover work in a cancellable coordinator, and publish one generation on JavaFX; test delayed multi-region jobs and close/recalculate.

area: SmallCoverWindow multi-region calculation retains only the last filled cover
linux_evidence: Found while following every rectangle through the fixed small_cover directory and loadCover replacement maps
windows_abdul_status: The UI accepts multiple rectangles and accumulates their outlines, but each native call overwrites the same small_cover artifact set and each Viewer load replaces stable/triple rectangle maps. After R1..Rk, durable proof artifacts and colored rectangles describe only the last successful region while earlier outlines may remain visible.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Return isolated per-region cover results or implement a native batch format, merge with explicit index/collision rules, and atomically publish one manifest-backed generation; test every region remains colored after a three-region batch.

area: SmallCoverWindow loses all accumulated outlines when the final raw line is skipped
linux_evidence: Found while tracing newSmallCoverAreas transfer under the raw loop index condition
windows_abdul_status: The shared outline list is cleared before calculation, but collected outlines are copied back only inside a nonempty successful result when i equals the final raw line index. A malformed/blank final line or erased native failure prevents transfer, discarding every earlier successful outline.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Parse accepted requests first and commit the complete collected list after whole-batch success, independent of raw input indices; test valid regions followed by blank/malformed/failing final lines.

area: SmallCoverWindow All mode silently overwrites the ordinary cover
linux_evidence: Found while tracing coverWrapperAll from LiCover to check_cover_all's hardcoded output directory
windows_abdul_status: LiCover All invokes the ordinary ALL-cover API, whose native destination is cover rather than small_cover. It ignores the covered Boolean, invokes no Viewer reload callback, saves, and closes, so an ordinary cover can be replaced while the screen and small-cover state remain unchanged.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Remove All from LiCover, implement a true small-cover ALL target, or explicitly present/reload the ordinary cover operation; test output ownership and covered false/true/failure.

area: SmallCoverWindow persists numeric input before raw parse and unchecked native ranges
linux_evidence: Found while tracing Calculate's save-before-parse ordering and signed Java values into unsigned native casts
windows_abdul_status: Malformed and overflowing values are first written to tmp, then wrapped in an uncaught RuntimeException on JavaFX. Negative and invalid-domain integers are not rejected before unsigned native conversion, whose exception coverage is incomplete at the C ABI.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Parse and validate named domains into locals before persistence or native entry; display field-specific errors and test malformed, overflow, zero, negative, and maximum accepted values.

area: SmallCoverWindow silently skips rectangle lines outside a literal single-space grammar
linux_evidence: Found while deriving polygonString.split newline and square.split single-space behavior
windows_abdul_status: Each line must yield exactly four tokens under split(' '); tabs, repeated spaces, wrong arity, and other malformed lines are silently continued. A partial or wholly empty batch can therefore close as successful without identifying omitted research regions.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Use a structured indexed rectangle parser over general whitespace, validate ordering/domain, reject the complete batch with line-numbered diagnostics, and test mixed valid/invalid inputs.

area: SmallCoverWindow re-cleans identical stable and triple lists for every rectangle
linux_evidence: Found while moving loop invariants out of the per-rectangle cost model
windows_abdul_status: cleanStables and cleanTriples are invariant across the rectangle loop but are called inside it. Each pass reparses/classifies every code and calls saveToDatabase, which may perform expensive MRR work, making preprocessing k times more expensive for k regions.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Source inspection and cost derivation in handbook Section 14.118
next_action: Clean once into immutable canonical text before the rectangle loop; benchmark many-region long-code workloads and retain deterministic input order.

area: StablesWindow runs Cartesian factor searches on JavaFX
linux_evidence: Found while tracing Calculate and Print Triple Combinations over p*q*r candidates
windows_abdul_status: Cartesian generation, terminal printing, parsing, missing-MRR materialization, database loads, and native factor removal all execute in JavaFX action handlers. Large lists freeze repaint/input/close for the full serial search with no progress or cancellation.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Preprocess unique codes once, evaluate indexed factor candidates in a bounded cancellable coordinator, preserve input order, and publish one generation on JavaFX; test delayed searches and close/recalculate.

area: StablesWindow pair count selects unrelated one-way triples
linux_evidence: Found by comparing current getCombinations with the numberOfPairs * 2 selection loop
windows_abdul_status: Reverse (bottom,unstable,top) generation was commented out in 2023, so workingTriples no longer contains adjacent orientation pairs. The pair field still selects the first 2n entries, which are merely different Cartesian candidates; zero, negative, and multiplication-overflow values can send an empty list.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Source inspection and indexed example in handbook Section 14.119
next_action: Select a literal validated triple count, or restore an explicit OrientationPair model grouped by canonical reversal; test n boundaries, overflow, and exact pair membership.

area: StablesWindow treats whitespace-only bottom stables as full-triple mode
linux_evidence: Found while tracing calculateAction's exact empty-string comparison before later trimming
windows_abdul_status: Half-triple mode is chosen only when bottomStablesText equals the zero-length string. Spaces/newlines select full mode, then trim to an empty bottom component and produce invalid Cartesian triples instead of the intended half-triples.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Use an explicit Half/Full mode control or a structured isBlank parse; test empty, spaces, tabs, newline, and valid bottom lists.

area: StablesWindow factor dispatch and parsers throw on malformed lines
linux_evidence: Found while tracing comma-count routing into pre-try array indexing and unchecked Optional/Either get
windows_abdul_status: Find Factors routes every non-three-component line to the half path; blank/one-component input indexes a missing component before the catch. Triple and half cleaners/checkers also call Optional.get and Either.get without validation, so bad code text escapes with raw exceptions on JavaFX.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Parse indexed lines into typed full/half records before dispatch, return all validation diagnostics, and never use unchecked get for user input; test every arity and invalid code/classification.

area: StablesWindow half-factor display ignores false and erases failures
linux_evidence: Found while comparing calculateHalfTriplesAction's Boolean check with showFactorHalfTriple
windows_abdul_status: The display path ignores a normal false result, while native code prints the candidate Factor even if no equation divides by it. It catches all parse/database/native exceptions as the same 'Half Triple Doesn't Work' message, so factor absence, empty code, invalid input, and backend failure are indistinguishable.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Return and display typed FOUND/NOT_FOUND/INVALID/EMPTY/BACKEND_FAILURE outcomes with indexed input and captured diagnostics; test a factor with and without divisible equations.

area: StablesWindow normal close persistence depends on mouse exit
linux_evidence: Found while comparing the VBox mouse-exit save hook with explicit Viewer shutdown close
windows_abdul_status: The Stage has no close-request handler. Edits are normally saved only when the pointer exits the layout; keyboard/system close paths need not emit that event. Abdul's explicit close saves main shutdown but does not define all direct window-close paths.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Source inspection and commit history in handbook Section 14.119
next_action: Install a Stage close-request save handler, retain idempotent explicit shutdown save, and test mouse, OS button, keyboard, programmatic, and main-window close paths.

area: StablesWindow materializes the same codes for every Cartesian triple
linux_evidence: Found by deriving saveToDatabase call count for top p, unstable q, and bottom r lists
windows_abdul_status: checkTriples issues three saveToDatabase calls for each of p*q*r candidates, although at most p+q+r unique input lines need materialization; the factor wrapper then reloads all three records per candidate.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Source inspection and cost derivation in handbook Section 14.119
next_action: Parse/deduplicate/materialize unique codes once, then run bounded ordered read-only factor checks; benchmark phase times and pool wait under fixed total worker budgets.

area: SuperPolyVary schedule does not round-trip and shares an incompatible bounds schema
linux_evidence: Found while comparing Super's nine-token writer with its constructor and PolyVary's six-token writer
windows_abdul_status: Super writes length maxima, side sums, and steps, but never reloads the first three length maxima. PolyVary shares the file and later truncates it to six tokens, deleting Super steps. Restarted schedules therefore depend on defaults and which dialog wrote last.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Source inspection and restart examples in handbook Section 14.120
next_action: Use a versioned complete Super request file or distinct schemas/paths, parse all fields atomically, and test both write orders across real process restarts.

area: SuperPolyVary rereads mutable dialog/global state during an active sequence
linux_evidence: Found while comparing the seven-value result tuple with every value read by the completion listener
windows_abdul_status: Only polygon and six base bounds are snapshotted. Each repetition rereads static Reps/steps/colors/AutoCover and live magnification/AutoSmall controls; Viewer mode/subdivision controls are live too. Reopening or overlapping runs can change termination, progress, search bounds, view, and publication mid-generation.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Create one immutable generation-tagged request containing every schedule/mode/publication value; active controllers must never read dialog controls or process globals; test reopen/edit and overlapping sequences.

area: SuperPolyVary calls showAndWait on an already visible Stage
linux_evidence: Found while tracing Viewer's stage.isShowing branch through unconditional getLoad
windows_abdul_status: The parameter Stage has no owner/modality, so the main Viewer remains interactive during its nested event loop. A second command detects the visible Stage and brings it forward, but then still calls getLoad/showAndWait, which JavaFX rejects for an already visible Stage.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: When showing, bring forward and return, or make one owned modal request with the command disabled until completion; integration-test rapid/reentrant invocation.

area: SuperPolyVary partially mutates global configuration before validation
linux_evidence: Found while tracing Load assignments and polygon/file operations in source order
windows_abdul_status: Color/cover flags and ten numeric statics are assigned one by one before complete validation. A later parse or polygon error leaves mixed old/new globals; zero/negative domains and repetition arithmetic overflow are not rejected, and result is assigned before persistence succeeds.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Parse polygon and all schedule fields into locals, validate domains and checked per-repetition arithmetic, persist atomically, then publish one immutable result; test failure at every field and file-write boundary.

area: Concurrent TetraBar runs share one Viewer intersection accumulator
linux_evidence: Found by scheduling two recursive Bar/Tetra success chains against Viewer.tetrahedronCodes
windows_abdul_status: Every run appends to and clears the same Viewer field. Non-modal overlapping runs can interleave result sets, intersect samples from different requests, erase another run's state, and then index a too-short list; JavaFX handler serialization does not provide generation isolation.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Source inspection and explicit two-Bar completion schedule in handbook Section 14.121
next_action: Move accumulation into an immutable-request controller or generation-keyed private state; disable/reject unintended overlap and test every controlled completion order, cancellation, and stale publication.

area: TetraBar reloads a different filename than it writes
linux_evidence: Found by comparing constructor read with Calculate persistence
windows_abdul_status: The constructor reads tetrahedron.txt only when the process static is empty, while Calculate writes tetrabar.txt. Same-process reopen appears to work through static memory, but submitted coordinates are not restored from the written file after a true restart.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Source inspection and restart contract in handbook Section 14.121
next_action: Choose one versioned request path/schema, persist atomically, and integration-test calculate then new-process reload including every field promised to round-trip.

area: TetraBar coordinate parsing is non-atomic and assertion-dependent
linux_evidence: Found while tracing split, assertion, numeric parses, and mutation order
windows_abdul_status: Literal split-space rejects tabs/repeated spaces, leading whitespace prevents comment recognition, and a normally disabled assert is the only arity check. Raw parse/index errors escape the JavaFX handler after lists are cleared and static defaults are partially published.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Source inspection and parser matrix in handbook Section 14.121
next_action: Parse trimmed indexed lines with a structured whitespace grammar into finite local values, report all diagnostics, then atomically publish defaults/results/persistence; test with assertions both on and off.

area: TetraBar accepts degenerate and non-finite stencil coordinates
linux_evidence: Found by deriving Tetra/Bar geometry for zero, signed, NaN, and infinite epsilon
windows_abdul_status: Zero epsilon repeats the center two or three times; NaN/Infinity in epsilon or input coordinates flows non-finite points into Vary/backend work; negative epsilon silently reverses sample orientation although no domain rule is stated.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Geometry derivation and edge-case analysis in handbook Section 14.121
next_action: Define a finite nonzero or finite-positive epsilon contract, require finite centers before launch, and test zero, signs, NaN, infinity, subnormal, and normal boundary values.

area: TetraBar sample tasks read live Vary controls from worker threads
linux_evidence: Found while tracing queuedVaryTask.call through its six boyanMenu TextField reads
windows_abdul_status: Each recursively launched background Task reads JavaFX controls off the application thread. Because the owner remains interactive, edits between samples can make one purported intersection combine Vary sets calculated under different bounds and cuts.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Source inspection and generation-consistency analysis in handbook Section 14.121
next_action: Snapshot and validate all Vary parameters on JavaFX into the immutable TetraBar request; worker tasks consume only plain values; test delayed samples with concurrent control edits.

area: Utils intersections use nested scans over code and String collections
linux_evidence: Found while tracing the pairwise TetraBar code fold and older BoyanMenu String intersection
windows_abdul_status: Both helpers scan every pair and perform ArrayList duplicate checks. TetraBar inputs are sorted sets, so Bar costs O(n*m) plus match checks and Tetra repeats that scan on the intermediate result; String comparison has the same nested shape.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Cost analysis in handbook Sections 14.121 and 14.123
next_action: Use deterministic two-pointer intersection for comparator-sorted code sets and a first-order-preserving indexed String intersection; benchmark long-result workflows and compare exact ordered outputs.

area: Updater replaces the live installation and reports success without checking child status
linux_evidence: Found by comparing waitFor with both scripts' premise that Java has exited
windows_abdul_status: Java waits for the child and never exits, while the scripts sleep then delete/sync the running app directory. Locked files or missing tools can yield partial replacement; Java ignores the exit code and unconditionally shows a downloaded/restart success alert.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Parent/child protocol trace in handbook Section 14.122
next_action: Stage and verify first, launch a trusted installer, shut Java down cleanly, have the installer wait for the actual parent PID, atomically swap with rollback, and report success only from durable verified evidence.

area: Updater does not authenticate or bind the executable artifact
linux_evidence: Found while tracing remote version text directly into unverified archive replacement
windows_abdul_status: HTTPS transport is used, but there is no signed manifest, trusted release key, digest/size verification, platform binding, or archive-entry validation before downloaded bytes replace executable files.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Require a signed manifest binding semantic version, platform, architecture, size, SHA-256, and artifact identity; verify bounded staging and safe archive paths before installer launch; define key rotation and rollback.

area: Updater allows concurrent destructive attempts through one temp path
linux_evidence: Found by overlapping two enabled-button raw Threads and the fixed BilliardViewer-update.zip path
windows_abdul_status: Every click and process can copy/replace/delete the same temp ZIP and start scripts that delete or rsync the same app directory. There is no state guard, unique staging directory, process lock, or ownership protocol.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Controlled overlap schedule in handbook Section 14.122
next_action: Use a single updater state machine, installation-wide lock, unique verified staging, disabled/reentrant UI policy, and cross-process tests proving exactly one installer can own the target.

area: Updater treats every version mismatch as upgrade and downloads an unbound fixed asset
linux_evidence: Found by substituting local-newer and formatting-different version strings
windows_abdul_status: Exact string inequality offers an update even when remote is older or incompatible. The remote version value is not used to select or verify the fixed Jar-tag archive, so advertised and installed versions can differ.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Parse a documented semantic version/channel policy with anti-rollback and bind the chosen target version to a signed platform-specific artifact manifest; test older/equal/newer/prerelease/malformed cases.

area: Updater network worker has no bounds, cancellation, or visible failure path
linux_evidence: Found while tracing openStream/readAllBytes from an untracked raw Thread
