windows_abdul_status: Version/artifact I/O has no explicit connect/read deadline or response-size policy. The non-daemon untracked Thread can keep the JVM alive after windows close; cancellation is absent and exceptions are printed only to a terminal a packaged GUI may not expose.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Thread and I/O lifecycle analysis in handbook Section 14.122
next_action: Use an owned cancellable task with bounded transport, progress/error states on JavaFX, shutdown integration, and injected timeout/truncation/oversize tests.

area: Utils tooltip timing setup is a no-op
linux_evidence: Found by tracing Main/Viewer setup call into the local unattached Tooltip
windows_abdul_status: setupCustomTooltipBehavior ignores all three parameters, creates one local Tooltip with hard-coded values, and discards it. The old global reflection is commented, so later toolTip instances do not receive the requested timing.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Apply an explicit timing policy to each actual Tooltip without private reflection and add a JavaFX test asserting installed show/hide/duration values.

area: Utils.verifyInfo evaluates empty metadata fields and always accepts reconstructed storage
linux_evidence: Found by tracing native copy_to_cinfoAll through InfoAll into verifyInfo
windows_abdul_status: Native load_info_all fills sinEquations/cosEquations and explicitly leaves leftRights/codeSeqLR empty. verifyInfo reads those empty fields instead of the commented equation fields, executes zero inequality loops for every point, and returns true; the caller's invalid-result delete branch is unreachable by equation failure.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Native-to-Java field trace and equation example in handbook Section 14.123
next_action: Use the correct typed equation fields only after defining finite/triple-count/tolerance/interior-proof contracts; return VALID/INVALID/INCONCLUSIVE evidence and integration-test distinct field fixtures before permitting database deletion.

area: Utils.runAndWait loses interruption and background action failures
linux_evidence: Found by comparing direct-FX and runLater/latch branches
windows_abdul_status: Off the FX thread, an action exception is raised only on JavaFX while the waiting caller returns normally. Interrupted await is ignored without restoring the flag and returns before the queued action necessarily runs, violating both the wait and cancellation contracts.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Branch and happens-before analysis in handbook Section 14.123
next_action: Use a Future/CompletableFuture result that transports action failure, restore/define interrupt behavior, handle JavaFX shutdown, and test delayed/throwing/interrupted actions without locks.

area: Legacy code-line cleanup corrupts or rejects valid OBO coordinates
linux_evidence: Found by executing trimCodeLine and isCoords on negative, exponent, integer, and whitespace variants
windows_abdul_status: trimCodeLine interprets every hyphen as display metadata: a leading negative coordinate indexes a nonexistent split element and an exponent like 1e-5 is truncated. isCoords then requires exactly one ASCII space and a decimal point in both tokens, misrouting integers, tabs, and repeated spaces into code parsing.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Worked String.split cases and parser contract in handbook Section 14.123
next_action: Replace heuristic normalization with one indexed sum-type parser for Coordinate/Code/Directive/Comment/Error, require exactly two finite doubles for coordinates, and preserve explicit legacy display formats with golden tests.

area: Shared Utils text persistence truncates in place and creates files during reads
linux_evidence: Found while tracing writeToFile/readFromFile guarantees across dialog defaults and cover state
windows_abdul_status: Writes use TRUNCATE_EXISTING with no staging, flush/atomic move, lock, or generation; crashes/full disks/concurrent writers can lose the previous valid file. Reads mutate the filesystem and collapse missing into empty, with an exists/create race and working-directory ambiguity.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Persistence contract and failure matrix in handbook Section 14.123
next_action: Use side-effect-free typed reads and locked versioned atomic sibling-temp replacement with durable close/move and documented fallback; test mid-write failure, concurrent processes, cwd changes, and restart recovery.

area: Formula diagnostics consume empty fields and can print a false lower bound
linux_evidence: Found by deriving the radius*Equation.bound margin and tracing Viewer's hidden formula handler
windows_abdul_status: Viewer passes leftRights/codeSeqLR that loadAllEquation leaves empty; the formula controls are not attached. Both formula methods seed the minimum with 1000, accept invalid radius/non-finite data, and treat a negative certificate margin as a highlighted result without structured INCONCLUSIVE semantics; the mrr-named method does not calculate MRR.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Mean-value derivation and call-path inspection in handbook Section 14.123
next_action: Build one pure parsed equation-margin engine returning CERTIFIED/INCONCLUSIVE/INVALID records, use sin/cos fields, positive-infinity/explicit empty folds, finite nonnegative radius and declared norm/units, then wire or remove the UI intentionally.

area: Utils.verifyVector parses inputs but always returns true
linux_evidence: Found by tracing the empty point loop and repository callers
windows_abdul_status: verifyVector reads points/vectorX/vectorY and then performs no operation inside its only loop before returning true. It has no current caller, so this is latent rather than an active false validation.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Source and no-caller inspection in handbook Section 14.123
next_action: Delete the stub until a cited vector invariant exists, or implement typed evidence with failing examples and tests before adding any caller.

area: Invalid CLI thread value discards a valid property or environment setting
linux_evidence: Found by evaluating configureThreadCountFromArgs after static property/environment initialization
windows_abdul_status: A present malformed or nonpositive --threads value calls parseThreadCount with defaultThreadCount as fallback, not the already valid numThreads. An accidental bad CLI value can therefore replace an explicit property/env budget with half the processors.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Configuration precedence example in handbook Section 14.123
next_action: Retain the last valid lower-precedence value or fail explicit CLI validation; test each source in fresh JVMs including flag-like/missing values and property write-back.

area: Utils.modN can loop forever for a nonpositive modulus
linux_evidence: Found by evaluating both repeated add/subtract loops at n equals zero or negative
windows_abdul_status: At n=0 neither loop changes value; for negative n the promised range is invalid and repeated updates can diverge. Current geometry callers normally supply positive sizes, but the public helper has no guard.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Require n>0 and delegate to Math.floorMod; test integer extremes, positive moduli, zero/negative rejection, and polygon construction invariants.

area: Continued-fraction helper lacks finite and convergence bounds
linux_evidence: Found by running the double recurrence for NaN, infinity, extreme, and near-zero values
windows_abdul_status: The private diagnostic helper uses double numerator/denominator recurrence with relative-only tolerance, no iteration/denominator/overflow guard, and NaN-sensitive loop comparison; it can print meaningless NaN/Infinity fractions or behave poorly on extremes.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Recurrence derivation and edge-case analysis in handbook Section 14.123
next_action: Use finite input and bounded integer/BigInteger convergents with absolute plus relative error and a structured approximation result; test zero, rationals, irrationals, subnormal/huge, NaN, and infinity.

area: VaryWindowL partially mutates shared bounds and has inconsistent malformed fallbacks
linux_evidence: Found by tracing constructor and Load assignments at each parse failure
windows_abdul_status: Short bounds records retain prior cross-window statics; a bad six-token record resets OSNO side-sum to 200 instead of the declared 150. Load assigns flags and six statics sequentially before all point/file validation, so a failed attempt leaves mixed configuration.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Parse an exact versioned local request with general whitespace and semantic domains, persist atomically, then publish all defaults/result together; test every token and file-write failure.

area: VaryWindowL one-point mode destroys the saved list and can return null or stale work
linux_evidence: Found by firing the shared Load handler from getPoints(onePoint=true) before result initialization/reset
windows_abdul_status: One-point mode replaces editor text and writes x y over the multi-point coordinate file. If bounds/coordinate/persistence fails, result is not cleared: first use returns null as Optional and later use can return the previous present Tuple, launching the wrong points/bounds.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: One-point success/failure lifecycle in handbook Section 14.124
next_action: Build a separate nonpersistent current-point request, initialize/clear result per generation, and return typed failure; regression-test saved list preservation, first failure, prior-success failure, and restart.

area: Reentrant VaryL one-point command can launch the same dialog result twice
linux_evidence: Found by nesting a one-point button action while a non-modal showAndWait list call is active
windows_abdul_status: The second command is allowed when one-point mode is selected, fires Load on the visible shared Stage, closes it, and launches. Closing also releases the first handler's showAndWait, which reads the same present result and launches another run with another executor pair.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Explicit nested-event schedule in handbook Section 14.124
next_action: Use one owned modal/generation controller or reject any active request; separate one-point construction from Stage state and test all rapid/reentrant command combinations for exactly one launch.

area: VaryWindowL navigation and recursive task indices refer to different coordinate lists
linux_evidence: Found by comparing cleanPolygon point construction with raw newline navigation and range arithmetic
windows_abdul_status: Submission removes blank lines/parentheses/commas; navigation counts and parses raw lines with literal spaces. Task idx+1 can display/move to another source line or throw. Backward wraps to end rather than the last start+k*step point, so it is not inverse when the range is nondivisible.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Worked raw/clean index and start=1 step=2 end=4 examples in handbook Section 14.124
next_action: Parse once into source-line point records, use one ordinal/source mapping throughout, and wrap over the same arithmetic progression; test punctuation/blanks and all range directions.

area: VaryL recursive tasks read live BoyanMenu controls across one run
linux_evidence: Found by tracing the seven-value dialog tuple through later varyTrianglesL calls
windows_abdul_status: The request snapshots points and six maxima but each worker/coordinate rereads JavaFX min/max/shots/type controls through BoyanMenu; override still rereads several controls. Main remains interactive, so one run can use different search definitions and performs off-FX control access.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Snapshot every search/range/type/publication value on JavaFX into one immutable request; workers consume plain values only; test delayed coordinates with live UI edits.

area: Static VaryL Override prevents entering the dialog that enables it and couples normal/middle windows
linux_evidence: Found by tracing Viewer's no-code-type guard before lazy window construction
windows_abdul_status: With no code types and default Override=false, Viewer rejects before showing the dialog, so a first-time user cannot select Override. Once either normal or middle submits, its shared static controls the other's eligibility regardless of that instance's displayed checkbox.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Pre-dialog gate and two-instance state trace in handbook Section 14.124
next_action: Always collect a local request before validating type/override compatibility and remove cross-window process statics; test first-use override and divergent normal/middle settings.

area: Nonempty LR text disables Viewer iteration validation without selecting an LR algorithm
linux_evidence: Found by tracing both iteration handlers from their size guards through pattern-array indexing
windows_abdul_status: Both handlers bypass all input-count and single-or-triple validation when the LR text area is nonempty, but no later branch consumes that LR text. The same pattern arrays are indexed; zero codes can reach deleteCharAt(-1) or get(0), and mismatches/gapped code arrays can fail or operate on unintended inputs.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Failure schedules and request-mode contract in handbook Section 14.125.3
next_action: Parse one immutable PatternMode or LeftRightMode request, validate packed code cardinality and every array/range before work, and add zero/gap/mismatch/nonempty-LR regression tests.

area: Viewer configures a large legacy command surface that is absent from both final scenes
linux_evidence: Found by tracing provisional HBox construction, later clear/reparent operations, and final BorderPane/codeWindow scene graphs
windows_abdul_status: Numerous fields receive labels, handlers, and sometimes persistence behavior but are never attached: iteration Draw Square/raw Expando/manual LR check plus Query, Classify, Gradient, LookAtMe, fill tools, trimmer, formula, Auto Vary3, policy toggles, range editors, and others. Hidden defaults still affect workflows, while maintainers may mistake configured handlers for shipped features.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Complete final reachability inventory in handbook Sections 14.125.2, 14.125.4, and 14.125.9
next_action: Decide intentionally per command: attach only validated feature-complete behavior with UI tests, or remove dead state/handler and update instructions; do not expose known unsafe latent paths wholesale.

area: Viewer Expando LR mode labels do not match implemented behavior
linux_evidence: Found by evaluating every LR radio branch and the unreachable manual checker
windows_abdul_status: The default No LR and Show LR choices both reach Invalid choice. Use LR with test performs only ordinary loadInfo calls and never predicts or compares LR values; comparison-like code exists only behind an unattached destructive button that deletes and recomputes database rows.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: LR recurrence, branch table, and side-effect analysis in handbook Section 14.125.4
next_action: Define explicit NoLR/ShowLR/Predict/PredictAndCompare strategies; make comparison side-effect-free with structured evidence, remove destructive database deletion, and test every displayed mode end to end.

area: Viewer Load Directory shares one info.txt section counter across every file
linux_evidence: Found by tracing readState allocation outside Files.walk forEach and the state-3 break
windows_abdul_status: After the first info.txt advances the shared counter through stable, triple, and finished markers, later files begin at terminal or higher states and load no normal sections. A malformed first file leaks its partial grammar into the next; traversal order therefore changes the loaded cover.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Per-file state transition and two-file failure schedule in handbook Section 14.125.8
next_action: Allocate a typed parser state per file, require/validate the expected marker order, report source path and line, and test two valid files plus malformed-then-valid order permutations.

area: Viewer recursively scans and parses Load Directory on the JavaFX action thread
linux_evidence: Found by following the button handler through both Files.walk passes, Files.lines parsing, classification, and drawCodes dispatch
windows_abdul_status: Large or slow directory trees perform all traversal and per-line work inline on JavaFX, freezing repaint, input, close, and cancellation; native/database drawing may add further delay and errors are mostly terminal-only or unchecked.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Run a cancellable background scanner that emits immutable path-ordered records, publish on JavaFX in bounded batches, and test delayed traversal, cancellation, malformed files, and close responsiveness.

area: Viewer Merge deletes the live cover before a replacement merge succeeds
linux_evidence: Found by tracing Merge ordering and ignored filesystem return values
windows_abdul_status: The JavaFX handler creates relative cover, deletes every existing child without checking success, then calls native merge with no confirmation, staging, manifest, rollback, or shared artifact lock. Failure or overlap can destroy the last valid cover or leave a mixed generation.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Failure schedule and BUG-055 composition in handbook Section 14.125.8
next_action: Merge into an isolated sibling generation under the common cover artifact lock, validate a complete manifest, atomically switch current generation, preserve rollback, and inject delete/native/crash/concurrent-writer failures.

area: Viewer Classify uses regex .txt splitting and truncates valid output names
linux_evidence: Found by evaluating String.split(\.txt\") where dot is a regex wildcard"
windows_abdul_status: A path such as file.txt can lose the character immediately before txt and earlier wildcard-plus-txt substrings can truncate more of the absolute path, so classification writes an unexpected stem or overwrites an unintended sibling.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Worked filename derivation in handbook Section 14.125.6
next_action: Use Path filename/extension operations with an explicit overwrite policy; test no extension, .txt, uppercase extension, multiple dots, parent names containing txt, and existing output.

area: Viewer silently ignores every iteration pattern after the first empty dimension
linux_evidence: Found by deriving iterateThru's base case and comparing it with constructor validation
windows_abdul_status: iterateThru treats an empty vectors[depth] as a leaf instead of skipping it. The UI permits pattern 1 nonempty, pattern 2 empty, and pattern 3 or 4 nonempty, but all later patterns are silently ignored and a smaller candidate lattice is calculated.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Vector equation and [P1,empty,P3] trace in handbook Section 14.127.2
next_action: Reject non-prefix pattern holes or compile nonempty pattern records into a dense immutable list; validate ranges/positions and test every empty/nonempty dimension combination.

area: Cancelling VaryL draws partial Storages even when Draw was disabled
linux_evidence: Found by comparing recurseDrawVaryL partial/success draw guards with its cancellation handler
windows_abdul_status: Normal partial and success publication checks draw, but onCancelled unconditionally adds every partial Storage to onScreenSequences and then renders. A no-draw research run changes the visible model only when cancelled.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Terminal-state publication table in handbook Section 14.128.2
next_action: Apply one immutable publication policy in success/cancel/failure paths and test draw true/false with zero/some partials at every terminal state.

area: Failed VaryLTask leaves its storage and shot executors running
linux_evidence: Found by comparing recurseDrawVaryL success/cancel/skip terminal branches with setOnFailed
windows_abdul_status: Success, cancellation, and terminal skip request shutdown of both per-run executors; failure only closes progress and throws from the FX callback. Fixed-pool threads and queued native/database work can survive the failed run and keep the process alive.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Failure branch and executor ownership trace in handbook Section 14.128.2
next_action: Give one run controller finally-equivalent ownership of child Futures and both executors, cancel/await asynchronously on every terminal state, preserve failure evidence, and inject worker/partial/publication failures.

area: Viewer window shutdown does not cancel or join per-operation work
files: src/java/billiards/viewer/Viewer.java; src/java/billiards/viewer/Updater.java; multiple Vary and Cover controllers; docs/CODEBASE-HANDBOOK.md
linux_evidence: Found by comparing closeChildWindowsForExit ownership with every raw Thread and per-operation executor created by Viewer
windows_abdul_status: Closing owned Stages and Main's long-lived executor does not cover update, Tetra, VaryL storage/shot, render, and other operation-specific workers. A visually clean close can permit late publication, native work against a destroying pool, or non-daemon threads retaining the process.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Window-versus-work ownership analysis in handbook Sections 14.126 and 14.128
next_action: Register every operation under one application lifetime with cancellation token/Futures/executors, stop admission on close, cancel and asynchronously join before pool destruction/Platform exit, and test close during every workflow.

area: Viewer scans external SQLite code tables synchronously on JavaFX
linux_evidence: Found by tracing PolygonDB LoadFileAction through JDBC connection, query iteration, row parsing, classification, and batching
windows_abdul_status: drawCodes batches are asynchronous, but opening each table and consuming every ResultSet row occurs inline in the button handler. Large, remote, busy, or malformed databases freeze repaint, close, and cancellation and expose failures only through terminal output or unchecked exceptions.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: JDBC thread-boundary analysis in handbook Section 14.129.2
next_action: Use a cancellable background reader with immutable bounded batches/backpressure and a typed per-table summary; test delayed/busy DB, malformed rows, cancellation, and close.

area: Viewer base-code and triple load Tasks race to shut down one shared draw executor
linux_evidence: Found by tracing the full drawCodes branch when singles and triples coexist
windows_abdul_status: Both concurrently submitted outer Tasks receive the same fixed inner executor, and every terminal handler independently shuts it down. Whichever Task finishes or is cancelled first can reject/cancel the other's inner Storage work, making mixed file/directory results scheduling-dependent.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Two-task completion schedules in handbook Section 14.129.5
next_action: Give each Task a private executor or one structured batch owner that shuts a shared pool only after both terminal states; test empty/small/large singles and triples in every controlled completion/cancel/failure order at 1/2/4 workers.

area: Viewer writes arbitrary three-code arrays into the Cover triple lane
linux_evidence: Found by tracing triple draw success from parsed ClassifiedCodeSequence[] to appendTriplesInfo
windows_abdul_status: When all returned components intersect, drawCodes appends their comma-joined text without verifying exactly three present Storages in stable-unstable-stable order. A malformed or wrong-type input triple can enter proof/cover artifacts as though structurally valid.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Triple admission analysis in handbook Section 14.129.5
next_action: Parse and calculate a typed CoverTriple requiring complete stable-unstable-stable components before any cover append; report invalid/incomplete records with source lines and test every type/cardinality/empty combination.

area: Viewer progress quota replaces close cancellation and permanently exhausts after normal completions
linux_evidence: Found by composing showProgressWindow with Progress.incrementWindowCount and Stage.close semantics
windows_abdul_status: Before show, Viewer replaces Progress's close-to-requestCancel handler with a counter increment. Normal task completion hides/closes programmatically without replenishing the counter; after five completions later tasks show no modal progress/cancel UI. Window-manager close also no longer requests Java task cancellation, compounding BUG-086.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Handler replacement and six-load schedule in handbook Section 14.129.6
next_action: Use a progress manager that composes rather than replaces cancellation, tracks active dialogs on every hidden/terminal path, and test more than five success/failure/cancel/user-close sequences with native cancellation.

area: Triple file-load publication rereads live code-type checkboxes after calculation
linux_evidence: Found by comparing launch-time parsing/type selection with drawCodes triple onSucceeded filters
windows_abdul_status: While DrawPictureTaskTriples runs, users can edit Boyan code types. Its completion callback uses those new selections to decide which stable components to draw/add, while table/file selection used the old state, so one batch has mixed configuration semantics.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Launch-versus-completion state trace in handbook Section 14.129.5
next_action: Capture one immutable selected-type set in the load request and use it for parsing, calculation, and publication; test every mid-run checkbox edit and overlapping load.

area: Visible Zoom always selects Boyan center mode and ignores interval maxima
linux_evidence: Found by composing the Zoom button handler with positionZoom's boyanRdoBtn branch
windows_abdul_status: The button selects hidden boyanRdoBtn immediately before parsing. positionZoom then takes its center-only branch even for nondegenerate bounds, preserves the old scale, and uses only xMin/yMin although the tooltip promises best-fitting interval zoom.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Affine fit derivation and visible-button trace in handbook Section 14.130.1
next_action: Pass an explicit Center or Fit camera command instead of hidden radio state; test wide/tall/square bounds through the actual button and Zoom To.

area: Viewer removes transparent on-screen Storages during keySet iteration
linux_evidence: Found by tracing click selection's enhanced loop over LinkedHashMap.keySet
windows_abdul_status: When a transparent entry is encountered, click calls onScreenSequences.remove inside the fail-fast enhanced iteration. A later iterator advance can throw ConcurrentModificationException and abort selection/rendering.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Selection-loop mutation analysis in handbook Section 14.132.3
next_action: Use Iterator.remove or a separate cleanup snapshot and test transparent entries at first/middle/last positions with mixed stable/unstable contents.

area: Overlapping Cover rectangles select an unspecified first HashTriple entry
linux_evidence: Found by tracing stable/triple/half-triple click loops through UnifiedMap-derived entry lists and unconditional break
windows_abdul_status: Each category stops after the first containing rectangle, but UnifiedMap encounter order is unspecified. A missing first Storage can also block a later valid overlap. Selection, recoloring, and shown MRR can change with hash/layout history rather than explicit z-order.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Define deterministic z-order/best-hit or return all hits with a stable comparator; test overlapping equal/different types, missing storage, insertion permutations, and reloads.

area: Nontrimable stable and real half-triple Cover rectangles cannot be selected
linux_evidence: Found by comparing render trimming semantics with click's explicit !rect.trimable continue
windows_abdul_status: A false trimable flag means use the whole rectangle rather than clip to a stable polygon during rendering, but selection rejects it unconditionally. Rectangle-visible cover entries therefore have no click result; real half triples repeat the rejection.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Trim-policy branch table in handbook Section 14.132.4
next_action: If trimable require polygon membership; otherwise accept rectangle containment. Test stable/half-triple true/false, inside/outside polygon, and synthetic half triples.

area: Click zoom accepts zero, negative, NaN, and infinite scale factors
linux_evidence: Found by following raw zoomScaleText parsing into scaleBy(z) and scaleBy(1/z)
windows_abdul_status: Zero yields scale zero or reciprocal infinity, negative factors reverse the affine map, and NaN/infinity contaminate pixel/radian coordinates and later geometry/rendering; no validation alert precedes mutation.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Require a finite positive factor within practical bounds before mutation, preserve the old camera on failure, and test all special doubles plus repeated extreme zoom.

area: Main Calculate performs native/database Storage work synchronously and reloads for Cover
linux_evidence: Found by tracing btnCalculateAction/buttonCalulator/calculateCurrentCodeNumbers and optional Cover publication on JavaFX
windows_abdul_status: Single/triple calculation loads or computes each Storage inline, blocking input/repaint/close. Add to Cover then reclassifies and reloads the same successful Storages instead of reusing results, increasing latency and permitting a second load outcome.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Direct Calculate pipeline in handbook Section 14.131
next_action: Parse atomically, calculate once in a cancellable background request, and publish/print/admit Cover from one immutable Storage result; test delayed native work, cancellation, partial triples, and load-call counts.

area: Viewer hole scans can pair an old image with a new camera map
linux_evidence: Found by composing pan/zoom asynchronous render submission with immediate findHole/findHoles reads
windows_abdul_status: Hole methods fetch the currently committed regions image but convert pixels through the live map. After a camera mutation and before its render commits, pixels describe the previous view while returned/tested coordinates describe the new view, producing false holes, fills, or AutoVary decisions.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Generation mismatch schedule in handbook Section 14.133.1
next_action: Bundle immutable map/generation with each rendered image and scan only a matching committed pair or an afterCommit snapshot; test delayed old/new camera renders and cancellation.

area: Viewer hole helpers return the same Vector2 type in different units
linux_evidence: Found by comparing findHole and findHoles return construction
windows_abdul_status: findHole returns radians while findHoles converts to degrees; names and generic Vector2 types do not expose the difference. Current single-hole caller uses presence only, but a value-consuming caller can double-convert or launch Vary at the wrong coordinate.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Use RadianPoint/DegreePoint types or one canonical unit with explicit boundary conversion; add compile-time/API and numeric conversion tests.

area: Coalesced Viewer rendering snapshots only regions and four toggles
linux_evidence: Found by enumerating every field read by redoFromScratch/buildRenderedImages after background submission
windows_abdul_status: Workers still read live PixelRadianMap, offset JavaFX TextField, fills, Cover maps/colors, bound collections/Optionals, Small Cover areas, MRR map, and OBO state. FX mutation can cause off-thread control access, ConcurrentModificationException, or one image mixing camera/data generations despite the final generation check.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Complete render-state inventory in handbook Section 14.134.4
next_action: Build one immutable RenderSnapshot on JavaFX containing all camera/options/numeric/ordered geometry/color/OBO state; worker code receives no Viewer/control references. Test mutation of every component during delayed builds.

area: Viewer Bounds coloring is immediately overwritten for every pixel
linux_evidence: Found by tracing consecutive PixelWriter writes in redoFromScratch
windows_abdul_status: When Bounds is selected, containing stable polygons write fillBoundColor, but the next unconditional statement writes the normal computed stable/transparent color to the identical pixel. The option adds O(SIDE^2*stable-count) work with no region-image effect.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Pixel write order in handbook Section 14.134.3
next_action: Define bounds as a separate overlay or compose color once with explicit priority; add pixel-hash fixtures proving selected versus unselected output differs as intended.

area: Viewer leaves render row Futures running after one row fails or submission aborts
linux_evidence: Found by comparing redoFromScratch Interrupted/Cancellation catches with ExecutionException and submit failure
windows_abdul_status: Interruption and cancellation cancel all rows, but ExecutionException immediately throws and a partial RejectedExecution path has no cleanup. Remaining expensive row jobs continue consuming the shared executor after their image can no longer commit.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Row-future terminal-state table in handbook Section 14.134.2
next_action: Own submitted rows in try/finally and cancel every unfinished Future on all non-success exits while preserving interrupt/root failure; inject early/middle/late row and submit failures.

area: Background Viewer render failures disappear when no callback is supplied
linux_evidence: Found by following a current-generation build RuntimeException through executor.submit and unobserved renderFuture
windows_abdul_status: With onFailure null the render Runnable rethrows, FutureTask captures the exception, and no caller gets/logs the Future. The old image remains with no UI or terminal evidence; rejection is likewise ignored without a callback.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Failure propagation analysis in handbook Section 14.134.5
next_action: Observe every render generation terminal state through a central logger/UI result, distinguish stale/cancel from failure, and test build/commit/callback/rejection errors.

area: Removing an unstable selected code leaves its MRR line overlay behind
linux_evidence: Found by comparing click-created stable/unstable mrrBounds with makeRightScrollPane X removal
windows_abdul_status: The X handler searches/removes an MRR polygon only for stable Storage. Triple and half-triple selection adds a degenerate unstable line polygon, but removing that row/Storage does not remove the bound, leaving stale visual state.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Selection-row ownership trace in handbook Section 14.133
next_action: Associate every row/selection with explicit overlay IDs and remove all owned overlays transactionally; test stable, triple, half-triple, duplicate geometry, and repeated select/remove.

area: Viewer render allocates and then replaces every inner color row array
linux_evidence: Found by comparing new Color[SIDE][SIDE] allocation with assignment of each Future-returned Color[]
windows_abdul_status: The two-dimensional allocation eagerly creates 600 row arrays, then colors[x] is replaced with another 600-element array from each task. A normal render transiently allocates two complete reference-row sets and discards the first.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Allocate Color[SIDE][] or one safely partitioned flat buffer; benchmark allocation rate, GC pauses, and pixel parity at 1/2/4 workers.

area: Viewer parseFile false-flag overload crashes on every triple
linux_evidence: Found by tracing tripleString construction under addToGarbage=false through unconditional deleteCharAt
windows_abdul_status: The builder is populated only when addToGarbage is true, but deleteCharAt(length-1) and triple persistence are unconditional. Classify and PolyTrimmer call parseFile(path,false), so the first triple attempts deleteCharAt(-1) and aborts the file.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Flag/caller matrix and exact failing statement in handbook Section 14.140
next_action: Make parsing side-effect free and condition the later atomic persistence phase on the flag; test triple files through main Load, Classify, PolyTrimmer, and both parser flag values.

area: Older overlapping unstable Viewer regions overwrite newer ones
linux_evidence: Found by composing redoFromScratch reverse insertion order with renderUnstable unconditional PixelWriter writes
windows_abdul_status: Unstable Storages are reversed to newest-first, but each is then painted over the same image. At an overlap, an older line runs later and overwrites the newer color. Stable coloring stops at the first newest match, so the two Storage kinds implement opposite priority.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Define one newest-wins compositor for stable and unstable pixels, or draw unstable oldest-first; add two- and three-line overlap pixel tests with insertion updates.

area: Loading an empty OBO or hole selection leaves the previous OBO image and Storage visible
linux_evidence: Found by following empty parse results through both OBO load paths
windows_abdul_status: Both paths replace fileCodeSequences and only update currentOBOStorage, image, and line text inside the nonempty branch. Empty input therefore leaves a visible old result that is not present in the newly loaded navigation list.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: OBO session state table in handbook Section 14.139
next_action: Publish entries/index/Storage/image as one session and explicitly clear on empty or failed replacement; test nonempty-to-empty, malformed, zero-count, and negative-count transitions.

area: Viewer parseFile can partially commit a failed file to the garbage database
linux_evidence: Found by comparing per-line triple writes with post-parse single writes and early error returns
windows_abdul_status: With addToGarbage true, each triple is saved immediately while singles wait until parsing finishes. A later bad line or unchecked parse exception leaves a committed triple prefix, omits singles, and returns failure, so retrying is not an atomic or idempotent import.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Parse and validate all typed records first, then persist singles and triples in one database transaction with rollback; inject failure after each record and assert zero partial writes.

area: Viewer iteration-file records index parallel metadata without arity validation
linux_evidence: Found by deriving parseFile plus/hash indexing for multiple comma-separated codes
windows_abdul_status: For each code index, every hash metadata section is split by comma and indexed at that code index. Any shorter section throws ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException; malformed records produce no line-specific format result and may follow earlier garbage side effects.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Parse plus records into typed arrays, require every section cardinality to equal the code count, and return a line/column error before side effects; test empty, short, exact, and long sections.

area: Viewer silently discards comma fields when an ordinary line has more than three sections
linux_evidence: Found by enumerating parseFile comma-count branches
windows_abdul_status: Exactly three sections means triple and exactly two means code plus color, but every other count takes only sections[0]. Four or more comma fields are accepted as one code while all trailing content is ignored, hiding malformed or unintended data.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Actual grammar table in handbook Section 14.140
next_action: Use explicit record variants with exact arities and reject surplus fields with line/column diagnostics; add 0/1/2/3/4/5-section golden tests.

area: Dormant Viewer varyLFunction treats max zero as no results and returns before FX additions
linux_evidence: Found by tracing the unused legacy collector's empty localCodesFound size and Platform.runLater publication
windows_abdul_status: When max is zero, the remaining counter is initialized from a newly empty set and the loop prints nothing. For positive max, accepted codes are queued onto JavaFX and the caller-supplied mutable set is returned before those additions execute, so the return is not a completed result.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Count and temporal contracts in handbook Section 14.141
next_action: Remove the dead path or return one immutable Task result with an explicit zero/unlimited policy and separate candidate/accepted totals; test FX callback reordering before any revival.

area: Failed direct PolyVary leaves its storage and shot executors running
linux_evidence: Found by comparing drawPolyVary success/cancel cleanup with setOnFailed
windows_abdul_status: Success and cancellation call shutdownExecutorAsync for both per-operation PriorityExecutors. Failure only closes progress, cancels Super, and throws from the FX handler, so queued/native/database work and pool threads can survive the failed operation and process lifetime.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Terminal-state ownership table in handbook Section 14.143
next_action: Give direct PolyVary one exactly-once terminal owner with finally-equivalent cancel/shutdown/await on success, cancellation, failure, and rejected submission; inject each failure boundary and assert no live child threads.

area: Direct PolyVary counts holes and advances Super before its full redraw commits
linux_evidence: Found by ordering drawPolyVary renderRegions submission, immediate findHoles, and step-property mutation
windows_abdul_status: The terminal callback schedules a coalesced redraw, immediately samples the current image for ending holes, and then starts the next Super repetition. Late partial publication or catch-up-only Storage may be absent; optional scaling can pair the next camera map with the old image. The fixed Auto coordinate barrier does not cover this direct path.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Make matching render commit a prerequisite for hole counting, completion reporting, and Super transition; test delayed render and delayed partial publication with camera scaling and stale-generation cancellation.

area: Cancelled grouped AutoPolyVary results cannot be force-printed after partial publication
linux_evidence: Found by composing processAutoPolyVaryStorage's on-screen dedupe guard with cancellation forcePrint
windows_abdul_status: Modes one/two suppress ordinary partial printing. Cancellation retries each partial with forcePrint=true, but every already displayed Storage returns at onScreenSequences.containsKey before the print branch, so completed grouped results can be drawn/saved without the promised terminal text.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Publication receipt trace in handbook Section 14.144
next_action: Track add/draw, terminal print, Cover, Small Cover, and iteration publication independently and idempotently; test cancellation before/after partial listener delivery in all modes.

area: Cancelling SuperPolyVary increments the subdivisions field once
linux_evidence: Found by tracing the step-property listener when a child changes its value from a repetition index to -1
windows_abdul_status: The listener applies Cycle Step whenever oldVal is not -1 before checking whether newVal is the cancellation sentinel. A cancelled/failed sequence therefore mutates live subdivisions as if another repetition completed, affecting the next manual run.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Listener transition order in handbook Section 14.142
next_action: Handle terminal sentinels before normal recurrence mutation and keep subdivisions in an immutable request rather than controls; test cancellation/failure from every repetition index.

area: Viewer autoRecurse generates every quadtree level rather than the advertised 4^max locations
linux_evidence: Found by solving the depth recurrence and comparing it with the source comment
windows_abdul_status: For an unpruned blank region, centers from depths zero through max produce (4^(max+1)-1)/3 pairs and twice that many boxed Double entries, followed by a second filtered list. At max 8 this is 87381 locations rather than 65536 leaves.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Closed-form recurrence and examples in handbook Section 14.145
next_action: Define whether multilevel centers or leaves are intended, stream typed points, pre-size when materialization is required, and benchmark count/time/allocation across subdivision depths and fill densities.

area: Direct PolyVary allocates every hole coordinate twice only to count them
linux_evidence: Found by tracing startHoles/endHoles findHoles(area).size calls
windows_abdul_status: Both completion metrics construct a Vector2 and append it for every transparent pixel in the polygon although only the list size is consumed. A mostly blank 600-square view creates hundreds of thousands of temporary objects before and after each run.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Add a count-only scan over a committed image/map generation or return aggregate raster statistics; benchmark allocation, GC, and count parity on empty/full/mixed images.

area: Viewer iteration transforms can leave partially mutated codes and mismatched persistence/UI
linux_evidence: Found by ordering addSubtract/increase/decrease component mutation, checked vector arithmetic, garbage save, resultant classification, and synchronize
windows_abdul_status: Patterns are parsed and applied in place one component/coordinate at a time before full validation. A later bad index, overflow, parse error, or database failure leaves earlier mutations; controls are synchronized only at the end. Invalid resultant codes can also be saved as pattern rows before classification returns.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Exact direction equation and transaction schedule in handbook Sections 14.146 and 14.148
next_action: Compile and validate one immutable transform and complete result first, persist with explicit status/transaction policy, then publish model and controls atomically; inject failure at every coordinate/save/classify boundary.

area: Navigating from an OBO code to a coordinate leaves the old code overlay active
linux_evidence: Found by comparing setOBO code and coordinate branches plus background OBO redraw
windows_abdul_status: The code branch assigns currentOBOStorage/image, while the coordinate branch only recenters and redraws. currentOBOStorage remains the preceding code and buildRenderedImages keeps rendering it in the OBO layer even though the selected line now denotes only a point.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: OBO transition table in handbook Section 14.147
next_action: Parse typed OBO entries and atomically publish index/camera/Storage/image, explicitly clearing Storage/image for coordinates; test code-coordinate-empty-code transitions and delayed redraws.

area: Nonfinite Viewer offset and move coordinates poison prover coloring and camera mapping
linux_evidence: Found by substituting NaN/infinities into getOffset, isPositiveProver, and point zoom
windows_abdul_status: NaN or negative-infinite prover offset makes every equation's rejecting comparison false, visually admitting all polygon points; positive infinity rejects all. NaN/infinite move coordinates make camera translations and later pixel/radian conversions nonfinite.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Numeric substitution in handbook Sections 14.148 and 14.152
next_action: Require finite parsed numbers at UI boundaries, preserve useful errors, and keep finite off-domain camera policy separate; test NaN, both infinities, threshold equality, and post-rejection map integrity.

area: Loading a new Viewer Cover retains half-triples from the previous Cover
linux_evidence: Found by comparing loadCover/loadCoverWithoutTrim map replacement with HashTriple category ownership
windows_abdul_status: Both loaders replace stable and triple maps but never clear or replace halfTripleMap. A later ordinary or small Cover is rendered and selected together with stale half-triple rectangles/colors from an unrelated prior run.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Five-file publication/state table in handbook Section 14.149
next_action: Parse one complete versioned Cover bundle and atomically replace every category, clearing absent ones; test ordinary/small/reload orders with preexisting half-triples and concurrent artifact replacement.

area: Queued Bar/Tetra Vary reads live JavaFX controls from its raw worker thread
linux_evidence: Found by tracing anonymous Task.call through six BoyanMenu TextField reads
windows_abdul_status: Each sequential point Task runs on a new raw Thread and directly calls getText/parse on six scene controls. This violates JavaFX confinement and lets derived points in one logical Bar/Tetra run observe different values if state changes between tasks.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Capture and validate one immutable numeric request on JavaFX before the first task, then give workers no control references; test edits during delayed multi-point runs and FX-thread assertions.

area: Cancelled or failed Bar/Tetra Vary leaves partial intersection sets for the next run
linux_evidence: Found by tracing the Viewer-field tetrahedronCodes across success groups and non-success callbacks
windows_abdul_status: Only a fully completed step-sized group clears tetrahedronCodes. Cancellation/failure after one or more point sets leaves them in shared Viewer state, and a later run can intersect new points with stale sets; disabled assertions do not enforce group ownership.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Group recurrence and terminal paths in handbook Section 14.150
next_action: Move point sets into an exactly-once run controller and clear/cancel/await on every terminal path; test failure/cancel after each point, immediate restart, and overlapping programmatic launches.

area: Bar/Tetra success performs candidate persistence and batch Storage loading on JavaFX
linux_evidence: Found by following queuedVaryTask Task-success handler through per-code saves and BatchLoadStorage
windows_abdul_status: After background candidate search, JavaFX synchronously saves every candidate set to garbage and, for drawable intersections, blocks while batch region loads finish. Large sets freeze input/repaint/cancel/close despite the visible task/progress structure.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Keep persistence and Storage calculation in cancellable background stages and publish one immutable result on JavaFX; benchmark large sets and test close/cancel during save/load with explicit partial-write receipts.

area: Native base file writes truncate live artifacts without atomic replacement
files: src/backend/cpp/basic.cpp; src/backend/headers/basic.hpp; multiple native cover writers; docs/CODEBASE-HANDBOOK.md
linux_evidence: Found by deriving std::ofstream default open mode and every caller's direct destination path
windows_abdul_status: open_file_write creates parents and opens the final text path directly, truncating the previous valid file before content is complete. Crash, disk-full/write exception, or competing writers can leave a torn prefix; there is no temp generation, durable close, checksum, lock, or atomic rename.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Primitive persistence contract in handbook Section 14.153
next_action: Provide versioned sibling-temp write/validate/durable-close/atomic-replace under the Cover artifact writer policy; inject open/write/flush/close/crash and concurrent-writer failures while asserting old-or-new content only.

area: Native split preserves empty fields where callers expect flexible whitespace
linux_evidence: Found by evaluating Boost is_any_of splitting with token compression disabled across numeric callers
windows_abdul_status: A delimiter string is a set of individual delimiter characters, and leading, repeated, or trailing delimiters produce empty tokens. Many numeric parsers pass one literal space and then index/convert fields, so visually harmless whitespace can change arity or throw instead of denoting the same record.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Exact token examples and caller inventory in handbook Section 14.153
next_action: Use explicit structured parsers: general-whitespace tokenization for numeric records, preserved empties only where schema requires them, and indexed diagnostics; add repeated/leading/trailing space/tab/CRLF golden tests at every format boundary.

area: Native ClassifiedCodeSequence exposes mutable shared input and independently mutable cached facts
linux_evidence: Found by tracing constructor caching, default shared_ptr copies, public CodeSequence::code_numbers, and all public derived fields
windows_abdul_status: After construction any caller can mutate the shared canonical word or independently overwrite length, sum, type, stable, and parity fields. Copies share the word but copy the caches, so one mutation can make each wrapper report a different internally inconsistent mathematical classification; CodeSequence's own cached type can also become stale.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Representation and copy semantics in handbook Section 14.154
next_action: Make the canonical word an immutable value or shared_ptr<const CodeSequence>, make all derived facts private read-only accessors, and construct only through validation; add compile-time immutability checks plus runtime copy/equality/classification consistency tests.

area: EquationGradient publicly exposes independently mutable equation and derivative cache
linux_evidence: Found by following public equation/diff0/diff1 members into gradient, Newton, intersection, and refinement consumers
windows_abdul_status: A caller can replace the stored equation without recomputing either partial, or alter one partial independently. Downstream code can then evaluate one boundary while using another boundary's normal or Jacobian, invalidating root convergence, intersection placement, and orientation decisions; immutability exists only by caller convention.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Typed cache construction and consumers in handbook Section 14.155
next_action: Make equation and both derivatives private immutable state with const accessors and only constructor/factory creation; apply the same invariant to RSOne/XYOne specializations and add compile-time mutation rejection plus Jacobian/equation consistency tests.

area: Integer symbolic differentiation multiplies and accumulates Coeff64 without a checked range
linux_evidence: Found by deriving each chain-rule coefficient product and following LinComMap collision addition
windows_abdul_status: For each trig term diff computes outer coefficient times X/Y frequency in signed 64-bit arithmetic, then may add it to an existing coefficient. Large generated code parameters can exceed the range; C++ signed overflow is undefined, configured builds may trap, and others can produce a corrupted exact equation used by every later gradient and proof step.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Chain-rule arithmetic boundary in handbook Section 14.155
next_action: Adopt checked coefficient arithmetic with explicit failure or arbitrary precision throughout symbolic maps, define accepted input bounds, and add product/collision cases immediately below/at/above both signed limits across supported compilers.

area: Evaluator narrows Coeff64 through signed-long GMP and MPFR APIs on 64-bit Windows
linux_evidence: Found by comparing Coeff64=int64_t with mpq_set_si/mpfr_mul_si signed-long parameters under LP64 and Windows LLP64/MinGW data models
windows_abdul_status: Full Equation X/Y/outer/bound coefficients and the EqVec aggregate bound are passed to APIs taking signed long. Values outside 32-bit range remain exact on typical LP64 Unix but narrow on 64-bit Windows before rational or directed-rounded evaluation; quadrant coefficient negation also cannot represent INT64_MIN.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Convert through checked intmax/GMP integer APIs or mpz intermediates without signed-long narrowing, reject unrepresentable negation, and run identical 2^31/2^32/2^63 boundary fixtures on Windows and LP64 while comparing certificate results.

area: Evaluator implicit copying shallow-copies owned GMP and MPFR handles
linux_evidence: Found by applying C++ implicit copy rules to mpz_t/mpq_t/mpfr_t array-handle members and the destructor's unconditional clear calls
windows_abdul_status: Evaluator declares a destructor but does not delete its copy constructor or assignment. An accidental copy duplicates internal limb pointers rather than numeric ownership, after which mutation aliases allocations and two destructors clear the same memory; current reference-based callers avoid but the public type permits it.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: RAII ownership analysis in handbook Section 14.156
next_action: Explicitly delete copy construction/assignment and either delete move or implement it only with GMP/MPFR swaps; add static_assert non-copyability and sanitizer-backed compile/runtime ownership tests.

area: Invalid-code helpers are declared inline but defined only in one cpp file
linux_evidence: Found by comparing the header declarations, cpp-only inline definitions, and absence of current cross-TU native calls
windows_abdul_status: C++ requires an inline definition to be reachable in every translation unit that odr-uses the function. A future native caller sees only declarations; compilers need not emit the unused cpp-local inline bodies, producing an undefined reference or ill-formed program despite the public header API.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Move definitions into the header or remove inline and emit normal external cpp definitions; add a two-translation-unit compile/link test plus exact native/Java diagnostic fixtures.

area: Cover merge replaces input target polygons with the full unit square
linux_evidence: Found by tracing union_covers from omitted load_polygon calls through hard-coded save_polygon and downstream consumers
windows_abdul_status: The quadtree union may combine covers of smaller, disjoint, overlapping, or otherwise different target polygons, but the output always declares the entire unit square. Java renders and consults that polygon as coverArea, and native CoverAll reloads it as the verification target, so artifact domain/provenance can claim space neither in the polygon union nor covered by leaves.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Structural union versus target-domain pipeline in handbook Section 14.158
next_action: Represent and serialize an exact polygon-set union, or require all input target polygons to be exactly identical and preserve that polygon; add disjoint/overlap/non-convex-union merge, viewer-domain, and native re-verification tests.

area: Missing trig closing parenthesis can wrap parse loop index and repeat indefinitely
linux_evidence: Found by tracing std::string::find npos through substring length arithmetic, assignment to the for-loop size_t index, and unsigned increment
windows_abdul_status: For input such as sin(x or cos(y, find(')') returns npos but the helper still parses the suffix and sets i=npos. The loop increment wraps to zero and repeats, continually adding the same term until hang or signed coefficient overflow rather than producing a bounded parse error.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Trig parser cursor schedule in handbook Section 14.159
next_action: Check closing-parenthesis presence before substring/index mutation and replace the scan with a full-consumption cursor parser; add timeout/fuzz tests for every truncated token and missing/extra parenthesis position.

area: Linear and trig parsers silently accept malformed text as a different formula
linux_evidence: Found by executing the index/length state machine for incomplete terms, adjacent symbols, repeated signs, and missing operators
windows_abdul_status: The scanners never require a pending coefficient/sign to end in a symbol and do not require separators between terms. Empty, signs, or digits can become zero; trailing digits are discarded; xy becomes x+y; 2+x drops 2; repeated signs and adjacent trig terms are accepted. Exact proof/test equations can therefore be changed rather than rejected.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Actual-versus-intended grammar tables in handbook Section 14.159
next_action: Implement explicit token grammars with complete cursor consumption and structured positions, retain canonical printer round trips, and add all malformed table cases plus property fuzzing that accepts a whole string or returns one bounded typed error.

area: PatternFinder database search performs the complete native table scan on JavaFX
linux_evidence: Found by following Search button action through Wrapper.search into row parsing, ordered grouping, and report allocation
windows_abdul_status: The active JavaFX handler synchronously scans and parses every row in the selected code-type table, builds all matches, and formats one potentially large string while holding a pooled connection. Large research tables block repaint, close, and input with no progress, paging, or cancellation.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Complexity and call-thread trace in handbook Section 14.160
next_action: Capture a validated immutable request on JavaFX, execute a cancellable/pageable query on a managed worker, publish bounded pages on JavaFX, and test delayed/large databases, cancellation, close, and connection release.

area: Code length and E/O searches parse every row because derived keys are not queryable
linux_evidence: Found by deriving both query plans from select code_sequence without WHERE predicates
windows_abdul_status: Every search reconstructs all CodeSequence values in the selected table even though entry length and parity signature are deterministic persisted-code properties. Runtime grows with the whole type table and all matching rows are retained before formatting.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Add transactionally maintained and indexed length/parity columns or a normalized search table, migration-verify them against canonical strings, compare query plans/results, and page output under a bounded-memory contract.

area: Cover trim publishes requested polygons outside the input target or root domain
linux_evidence: Found by tracing trim_cover's omitted input load_polygon and lack of polygon/root containment checks through output save and consumers
windows_abdul_status: The visitor can only retain leaves inside the saved root square, but trim_cover saves any requested convex polygon unchanged. A partial/outside/containing request can therefore declare target area the tree cannot represent and that the input never proved; Java coverArea and native CoverAll consume the inconsistent domain.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Visitor recurrence and target/root artifact pipeline in handbook Section 14.161
next_action: Load the input target and require requested subset of input target and root, or serialize the exact domain intersection as a polygon set; add equal/inside/crossing/outside/containing viewer and native re-verification fixtures.

area: Native cyclic run-length conversion indexes empty vectors before validation
linux_evidence: Found by evaluating convert and getCodeType first/last access for length zero before their optional/factory paths
windows_abdul_status: convert reads newCode[0] and newCode[len-1], while getCodeType calls front/back; neither checks empty first and the latter try block starts later. An empty direct or edge-path candidate invokes undefined behavior inside native or worker code instead of returning none/typed invalid.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Cyclic boundary alignment in handbook Section 14.162

area: Native worker-count paths accept trailing junk and unbounded positive counts
linux_evidence: Found by following strtol end/range handling and direct backend_set_worker_threads values into Boost pool sizes and TBB global_control
windows_abdul_status: BILLIARDS_NATIVE_THREADS accepts a numeric prefix such as 4junk, ignores ERANGE, and casts long to unsigned; the direct ABI accepts any positive int32 without a hardware/resource cap. Native-only scripts or callers can request enormous pools, exhaust threads/memory, and diverge from Java's clamped policy.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
next_action: Require complete checked numeric parsing, reject ERANGE and values above one documented native cap, align Java/native diagnostics, and test junk, signs, whitespace, 0, hardware boundaries, LONG/UINT overflow, and pool-creation failure.

area: Bezier Newton admissibility loop can run forever for empty or degenerate tangent boxes
linux_evidence: Found by tracing nonpositive tangent-intersection parameters into the 0-to-max predicate and step-halving loop
windows_abdul_status: cubic_bezier never requires both tangent-line parameters to be positive. A negative max makes 0<=coordinate<=max impossible while the initial point is already outside; halving a Newton step tends back to that inadmissible point, so the inside loop has no successful scale and no iteration limit. Zero-width coordinates can behave similarly.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Empty-box proof and Newton call contract in handbook Section 14.164
next_action: Reject nonfinite/nonpositive maxima before Newton, require the initial point inside a nonempty closed box, bound line-search iterations, return a typed failure, and test backward rays, zero widths, empty boxes, singular Jacobians, and cancellation/timeouts.

area: Bezier code treats uncertain or parallel endpoint tangents as proof that the implicit arc is a line
linux_evidence: Found by comparing parallel's interval-sign semantics with the straight-control fallback and the geometry of implicit curves
windows_abdul_status: sign(det)==ZERO means the determinant interval contains zero, not that all represented tangents are parallel. Even exact parallel endpoint tangents do not imply the intervening zero-set is straight. The fallback returns collinear trisection controls, which can leave the implicit curve except for a genuine line.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Determinant counterexample and straight-line worked example in handbook Section 14.164
next_action: Separate proven parallel from indeterminate interval signs, detect an actually affine implicit equation before using a line, otherwise refine endpoints or use a bounded interpolation method; test curved arcs with parallel tangents and determinant intervals that merely straddle zero.

area: Bezier headers define externally linked ordinary functions and specializations in every including translation unit
linux_evidence: Found by classifying header definitions as templates versus ordinary functions and explicit specializations

area: Chord-section Bezier interpolation has no rule proving Newton selected the intended implicit-curve branch
linux_evidence: Found by enumerating intersections of each unbounded chord-normal section with a trigonometric zero set and tracing the always-true admissibility predicate
windows_abdul_status: Each one-third/two-thirds section can have zero tangent one or multiple roots. Newton starts at the chord point but has no polygon side distance component or arc-order constraint, so the two solves can converge to remote or inconsistent branches. Equal endpoints create a zero line equation and singular Jacobian.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code

next_action: Define an arc-local bracket from verified geometry, isolate all section roots with interval methods, select one by proven topology/order, reject degenerate chords and ambiguous roots, and test zero/tangent/multiple intersections plus whole-curve error before display activation.

area: falgo zip cannot deduce its advertised second element type
linux_evidence: Found by applying C++ function-template deduction to both parameter types and the return-only S parameter
windows_abdul_status: zip declares both inputs as vector<T> while returning pair<T,S>. S occurs only in the return type, which is not a deduction context, so an ordinary zip(first,second) does not compile. Explicit template arguments accept two T vectors and convert the second elements instead of supporting vector<S>.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Signature expansion and intended truncated example in handbook Section 14.168
next_action: Change the second parameter to const vector<S>&, reserve the shorter size, and add same/heterogeneous type, empty, unequal-length, noncopyable policy, and compile-time deduction tests before use.

area: falgo append relies on an unenforced no-self-alias vector insertion precondition
linux_evidence: Found from the source comment and vector range-insert iterator invalidation contract
windows_abdul_status: append(a,a) passes iterators from the destination into its own range insertion. Reallocation/insertion can invalidate those iterators and the operation violates the helper's stated precondition; no type or runtime guard prevents a future caller from invoking undefined behavior. There are currently no production callers.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code

next_action: Detect address alias and append through a snapshot or reject it explicitly, reserve the total nonaliased size once, and add self-alias, repeated-alias, throwing-copy, empty, and multi-input tests.

windows_abdul_status: min_rotation is O(n^2) time and O(n) space; Stable materializes StableRef records and then copies parallel labels points and equations. CodeSequence already uses the linear least-rotation strategy delivered by OPT-002, but stable result serialization still uses the older algorithm.

windows_abdul_status: project_x(Rectangle) returns rect.interval_x and project_y returns rect.interval_y, but Rectangle exposes interval_x() and interval_y() and stores differently named private fields. Template definition parsing does not diagnose the dependent lookup; any call that instantiates either overload fails to compile. Current rectangle-polygon SAT projects the polygon on coordinate axes and can leave the defect hidden.

area: Manual Cover square selection stops refining on every exact dyadic boundary and silently returns a larger square
linux_evidence: Found by composing strict open child containment with calculateRectangle's unchanged-parent fallback
windows_abdul_status: Each subdivision child excludes its edges. A point such as x=pi/4 belongs to no root child, so calculateRectangle returns the root at that and every remaining requested magnification; root-edge, outside, NaN, and negative-depth inputs also yield an oversized square. Viewer marks it nontrimable and can attach the submitted code without reporting achieved depth.
fix_or_plan: Documented source behavior without changing production code
validation: Exact dyadic derivation and active manual draw-square trace in handbook Section 14.179
next_action: Validate finite in-domain coordinates and nonnegative bounded depth, select halves by a documented boundary convention or exact dyadic index arithmetic, return requested/achieved depth as typed data, and test midpoint/root-edge/outside/NaN plus all quadrant boundaries.
